You are a Lean 4 formalization agent in the Archon Horizon workspace `JacobianChallenge`, project `Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge` at /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge (all shell commands run from there). Mathlib checkout for grepping: /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.lake-packages/mathlib. The project builds green (all oleans fresh).

# Mission
Create ONE new file `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotFlatBaseChange.lean` and prove in it, sorry-free, the following theorem with EXACTLY this fully-qualified name and signature (it currently sits as a typed `sorry` in `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotFunctorDef.lean:414`; at integration the statement will be DELETED there and your file imported instead — namespace `AlgebraicGeometry`, then `Scheme`, universe `u`):

```lean
theorem CoherentSheafFlat.of_isPullback
    {X S X' S' : Scheme.{u}} {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g) (F : X.Modules) (hqc : F.IsQuasicoherent)
    (hF : CoherentSheafFlat f F) :
    CoherentSheafFlat f' ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F)
```

so fully qualified `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat.of_isPullback`. Read `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotFunctorDef.lean` lines 1–110 and 408–420 (context + the doc comment to adapt), and the definition:

`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat` (`AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean:1416`):
```lean
def CoherentSheafFlat {X S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
  ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) {V : X.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
    letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V) := Module.compHom _ (f.appLE U V e).hom
    Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V)
```
So the goal, unfolded: for every affine U' ⊆ S', affine V' ⊆ f'⁻¹ᵁU', the section module Γ(g'^*F, V') is flat over A' := Γ(S',U') where A' acts through `Module.compHom` along `(f'.appLE U' V' e).hom`. NOTE the action instance is a `letI`-`compHom` — every flatness transfer must land on exactly this instance (use `Module.Flat` transfer along ring-action identifications; the pattern `letI := Module.compHom ...; haveI : IsScalarTower ... := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)` appears in `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean` around lines 3495–3515 — read it, and grep FlatteningStratification.lean / FlatteningStratificationUniversal.lean / GenericFlatnessGeometric.lean for how existing proofs CONSUME and PRODUCE `CoherentSheafFlat`-style compHom flatness — there is very likely reusable bridging API there, e.g. how `flatLocus` proofs handle appLE-actions).

# Verified-available toolbox (all names checked in this checkout)
Mathlib `Mathlib/RingTheory/Flat/Localization.lean`:
- `Module.flat_of_isLocalized_span (s : Set S) (spn : Ideal.span s = ⊤) (Mₛ) ... (g : ∀ r : s, M →ₗ[S] Mₛ r) [∀ r : s, IsLocalizedModule.Away r.1 (g r)] (H : ∀ r : s, Module.Flat R (Mₛ r)) : Module.Flat R M` — MIXED two-ring criterion: M is an S-module (S an R-algebra... check: it needs [Module R M][Module S M][IsScalarTower R S M]-style setup per the variable block at lines 28–105 — READ IT). This is the keystone: flatness of Γ(g'^*F,V') over A' checked after localizing at a spanning set of B' := Γ(X',V').
- `IsLocalization.flat : Module.Flat R S` for a localization ring; `instance Localization.flat [Module.Flat R S] (p : Submonoid S) : Module.Flat R (Localization p)`; `Module.flat_iff_of_isLocalization`.
- `Module.Flat.trans`, `Module.Flat.baseChange` (Flat/Stability.lean), `Module.Flat.of_linearEquiv` / `Module.Flat.equiv`-style transfer (grep exact name).
Project engine (`AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean`):
- `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen (F) (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (b : Γ(X,V))` : `IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers b) (restrictBasicOpenₗ F b)` for quasi-coherent F (grep exact statement ~line 3400–3500). Also `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isCompact` (qcqs opens, :3441).
- Quasi-coherence of the pullback: `pullback_isQuasicoherent` in `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean` (01BG, arbitrary morphisms — grep exact name/signature).
Mathlib geometry:
- `AlgebraicGeometry.exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter` (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean:750`): for affine opens U,V and x ∈ U ∩ V there are f,g with `X.basicOpen f = X.basicOpen g ⊆ U ∩ V` a neighborhood of x — THE tool to get opens basic in BOTH of two affines.
- `IsAffineOpen.exists_basicOpen_le` (AffineScheme.lean:622).
- Pullback affine covers: `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Pullbacks.lean` (`Scheme.Pullback.openCoverOfLeftRight`, etc.) if useful.
Project FBC tilde dictionary (for the section identification):
- `pullback_spec_tilde_iso (φ : R ⟶ R') (M : ModuleCat R) : (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M) ≅ tilde ((ModuleCat.extendScalars φ.hom).obj M)` — `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean:704`.
- `pullbackRestrict_iso_tilde (F) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) {V} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) : (pushforward hV.isoSpec.hom).obj ((pullback V.ι).obj F) ≅ tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj ...)` — `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:419`; plus `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`, `pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh` nearby.
- The affine base-change heart kit (all sorry-free, grep in `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/`): `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`, `restrictedCartesianAffinePushout`, `isPushout_of_isPullback_SpecMap` (converse via `IsPullback.of_map_of_faithful` on `Scheme.Spec`), `coverInterOpen_baseChange_restrictedMap_comm`, `pullbackRestrict...` — session-0011 recipe: paste the restricted affine square onto the ambient square with `IsPullback.paste_vert`, convert to a ring pushout, identify corner sections. Also `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`, `Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr`, `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback` for functor-level compatibilities (see the iso chain at QuotFunctorDef.lean:342–350 for the canonical way to relate `pullback` and `restrict`).
- `Scheme.Hom.appLE` composition lemmas: `Scheme.Hom.appLE_comp_appLE`, `Scheme.Hom.appLE_map`, `Scheme.Hom.map_appLE` (Mathlib Scheme.lean — grep).

# Mathematical route (Stacks 00HB/01U9 affine-local; deviate if you find better, but this is checked against the API above)
Fix affine U' ⊆ S', affine V' ⊆ f'⁻¹U'. A' := Γ(S',U'), B' := Γ(X',V'), M := Γ(g'^*F, V') (a B'-module; A' acts via compHom appLE — set up `IsScalarTower A' B' M` via `of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)` after expressing the A'-action as compHom of algebraMap ∘ ... appLE composition: `f'.appLE U' V' e = algebraMap`-bridging; concretely make B' an A'-algebra via `(f'.appLE U' V' e).hom.toAlgebra` and check the module structures line up definitionally).

1. **Good cover**: For each x ∈ V', pick: affine U ⊆ S containing (f ∘ g')(x)=(g ∘ f')(x); affine V ⊆ X with g'(x) ∈ V ≤ f⁻¹U (shrink inside f⁻¹U using `IsAffineOpen.exists_basicOpen_le` on any affine nbhd); then U'' := a basic open of U' with f'(x) ∈ U'' ≤ U' ⊓ g⁻¹U (basic in U' so that Γ(S',U'') is a localization of A' — this keeps the final flatness OVER A' reachable via `Localization.flat`-type transfer; note: flatness over a localization of A' + ring-flatness of A' → A'_u gives flatness over A' by `Module.Flat.trans`). Then the open P := g'⁻¹V ⊓ f'⁻¹U'' ⊆ X' is an AFFINE open of X' isomorphic to the fibre product: restrict the ambient pullback square sq to (V, U, U'') and use the affine-pushout kit (`restrictedCartesianAffinePushout` et al.) — the project's FBC files already construct exactly this restricted affine square; find and reuse (grep for `coverInterOpen`, `restrictedCartesian`, and look at how `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean` builds per-σ base-change squares). Γ(X',P) ≅ Γ(X,V) ⊗_{Γ(S,U)} Γ(S',U'') as A'-algebras (ring pushout).
2. **Section identification on P**: Γ(g'^*F, P) ≅ Γ(F,V) ⊗_{Γ(X,V)} Γ(X',P) ≅ Γ(F,V) ⊗_{Γ(S,U)} Γ(S',U''): restrict g'^*F to P, identify `(g'^*F)|_P ≅ (pullback of F|_V along the restricted morphism P → V)` via the pullbackComp/pullbackCongr/restrictFunctorIsoPullback chain (same shape as QuotFunctorDef.lean:342–350), then apply the tilde dictionary: F|_V ≅ tilde M_V (qcoh at affine V — `pullbackRestrict_iso_tilde`/`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`), pullback of tilde along Spec-map = tilde of extendScalars (`pullback_spec_tilde_iso`), and global sections of tilde = the module. Extract from this an A'-MODULE identification of Γ(g'^*F, P) with `Γ(F,V) ⊗_{Γ(S,U)} Γ(S',U'')` (or with `extendScalars`-objects — whatever composes; keep careful track of which ring acts through which map; the `change`-to-native-action + `rw [← hact]` recipe below).
3. **Flatness on P**: hF at (U,V) gives Γ(F,V) flat over Γ(S,U) (compHom action). Then `Module.Flat.baseChange` (+ action bookkeeping) gives Γ(F,V) ⊗_{Γ(S,U)} Γ(S',U'') flat over Γ(S',U''); and Γ(S',U'') is a localization of A' (basic open of affine U'), so `Module.Flat.trans` (A' → Γ(S',U'') flat by `IsLocalization.flat`) gives flatness over A'. Hence Γ(g'^*F, P) is A'-flat.
4. **Both-basic refinement**: cover V' by opens Wₓ = basic in BOTH V' and P (via `exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter` at x ∈ V' ∩ P). Γ(g'^*F, Wₓ) is a localization of Γ(g'^*F, P) (basic in P, `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen` — needs (g'^*F).IsQuasicoherent from `pullback_isQuasicoherent`); "localized module of an A'-flat module is A'-flat": prove the mini-lemma `Module.Flat R M → Module.Flat R (LocalizedModule p M)`-shape (M an S-module, p : Submonoid S, R acting through S) — FIRST search Mathlib (loogle `Module.Flat, IsLocalizedModule`); if absent, prove via `IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange` (LocalizedModule ≅ Localization p ⊗_S M) + associativity-of-tensor flatness transfer, or mimic the `IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor` trick used in `flat_of_isLocalized_maximal`'s proof (same file, read it). Alternatively dodge: since Wₓ is basic in P, Γ(g'^*F,Wₓ) ≅ (M_P)_c ≅ M_V ⊗_{Γ(S,U)} (Γ(S',U'')-algebra (Γ(X',P)_c))-hmm — cleanest is: Γ(g'^*F,Wₓ) is ALSO computable as Γ(F,V) ⊗_{Γ(S,U)} <a ring flat over A'> — tensor-flatness argument once more. Choose whichever composes; the mini-lemma is the more reusable brick.
5. **Assemble with `flat_of_isLocalized_span`**: Wₓ basic in V' means Wₓ = V'.basicOpen bₓ for bₓ ∈ B'; the sets {bₓ} span ⊤ in B' — wait, they cover Spec B' = V' topologically, so `Ideal.span {bₓ} = ⊤` (standard: an affine covered by basic opens; Mathlib `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen_union_eq_self_iff` / `TopologicalSpace.Opens.iSup...` — grep for how the project or Mathlib converts "basic opens cover affine" into "span = ⊤": e.g. `IsAffineOpen.self_le_basicOpen_union_iff` or via `PrimeSpectrum.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top_iff...`). Then apply `Module.flat_of_isLocalized_span` with g r := the restriction maps `restrictBasicOpenₗ` and the `IsLocalizedModule.Away` instances from `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`, and H from step 4. Land the result on the exact compHom instance of the goal (a final `Module.Flat`-transfer along the identical carrier with `exact` should be definitional if you set the algebra structures up as `(...).hom.toAlgebra`; otherwise use the `change`-native-action trick).

# Non-negotiable constraints
- Edit/create ONLY `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotFlatBaseChange.lean`. NEVER edit any existing file. No scratch files left behind.
- Do NOT run bare `lake build` (concurrent agents share the build dir). Verify with `lake env lean AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotFlatBaseChange.lean` (foreground; compiles against existing oleans — safe).
- Do NOT import `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotFunctorDef` (cycle). Allowed: `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.FlatteningStratification` (for CoherentSheafFlat), `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme`, `AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.*`, Mathlib.
- Final: temporarily append `#print axioms AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat.of_isPullback`, record output (must be `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`, no sorryAx), then remove it.

# Lean v4.31 project gotchas (hard-won; respect them)
- Pin ONE universe for any auxiliary ModuleCat/SES-style algebra lemma (`Type v` binders, not `Type*`) — mixed universes produce raw `Quot ⇑(OreLocalization.oreEqv ...)` instance-goal garbage.
- `set S := Submonoid.powers ...` BREAKS instance unification in localized types — write submonoid terms explicitly everywhere; `set`/`let` for MAPS (data) is fine, for types/instance-carrying terms is not.
- Give every LinearEquiv in an equiv-chain a fully-typed `have`/`letI` — `refine LinearEquiv.trans ?_ e` with an open source type sticks on RingHomCompTriple metavariables. `TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange` + `Module.Free/Flat.of_equiv`-style transfer patterns: see the run-0010 GenericFreeness recipes inside `FlatteningStratification.lean` (§GenericFreeness) — READ that section's proofs first; it solved very similar two-localization/base-change transports (e.g. `(IsLocalization.Away.awayToAwayRight f g).toAlgebra` + `IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq (awayToAwayRight_eq ..).symm`).
- `IsLocalizedModule.map/map_injective/map_exact` at `mkLinearMap`s; upgrade R-linear→Loc-linear with `LinearMap.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization` (coe unchanged ⟹ Injective/Surjective/Exact transfer by defeq).
- `Module.compHom` action vs algebraMap in `map_smul'` goals: `change` to the native-action form `(ringmap r) • x`, then rewrite the ring-map equality (`hact : appLE ... = ...`).
- `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp; pass explicitly (+ `_assoc`) after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- `letI`-fvar IsIso instance goals: `inferInstanceAs (...)`, not rw-then-infer.
- `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` BEFORE the docstring cures instances-transparency walls; scope `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in` per declaration.
- Build section-object homs via `ConcreteCategory.ofHom { toFun := .., map_add' := .., map_smul' := .. }` with the expected hom type ascribed (never `ModuleCat.ofHom` on an unfolded carrier).
- Search hard before proving: grep the Mathlib checkout and `AlgebraicJacobian/`; lean-lsp MCP tools (load with ToolSearch "select:mcp__lean-lsp__lean_goal,mcp__lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages,mcp__lean-lsp__lean_local_search,mcp__lean-lsp__lean_hover_info,mcp__lean-lsp__lean_multi_attempt") for tight iteration on YOUR file; `lean_loogle`/`lean_leansearch` for Mathlib (rate-limited).

# House style
- Header/docstring style of QuotScheme.lean/QuotFunctorDef.lean; blueprint node `lem:coherent_flat_base_change` (complete LaTeX proof exists — grep `coherent_flat_base_change` under `blueprint/`; keep your Lean proof aligned with it, and note in your report if the Lean route materially differs so the blueprint can be updated).
- Intermediate lemmas (the mixed localized-flat mini-lemma, the affine-pullback-open sections iso, the both-basic cover extraction) should be public, well-named, reusable.
- This is a multi-hour task; persist. If ONE sub-piece truly won't yield after serious attempts, isolate it as a single named typed `sorry` lemma with honest docstring so everything else compiles — but sorry-free is the goal.

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GenericFlatnessGeometric
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GlueDescent
9	
10	/-!
11	# The Quot functor and the relative Grassmannian functor (real definitions)
12	
13	This file replaces the typed-`sorry` bodies of the two headline *definitions*
14	of the A.2.b Quot-scheme chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`)
15	with real constructions:
16	
17	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor` (`def:quot_functor`) — the Quot
18	  functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} : (Sch/S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (u+1)` of `T`-flat, finitely
19	  presented quotients `q : E_T ↠ F` on `X_T = X ×_S T` with proper support
20	  and fibrewise Hilbert polynomial `Φ`, modulo `ker q = ker q'`;
21	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian` (`def:grassmannian_scheme`) — the
22	  relative Grassmannian functor `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (u+1)` of
23	  rank-`d` locally free quotients of `V_T` on `T`.
24	
25	Universe note: the value category is `Type (u+1)`, not the `Type u` of the
26	iter-176 scaffold — a sheaf of modules on a scheme in `Scheme.{u}` is a large
27	object, so the quotient sets live one universe up.  This is the same forced
28	correction documented for the merged absolute Grassmannian functor
29	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`, `GrassmannianQuot.lean`) and for
30	`picSharp` (`FGAPicRepresentability.lean`); `Functor.RepresentableBy` is
31	universe-polymorphic, so the representability statements are unaffected.
32	
33	## Design
34	
35	A single family of quotients over `T : Over S` is the structure
36	`Scheme.QuotFamily π L E Φ T` (Nitsure §1, "family of quotients of `E`
37	parametrised by `T`"): a sheaf `F` on the relative product
38	`X_T := pullback π T.hom` that is finitely presented
39	(`SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation` — the coherence encoding used by the
40	flattening-stratification input), flat over `T`
41	(`Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π T.hom) F`), with proper support
42	(`Scheme.Modules.HasProperSupport`), together with an epimorphism
43	`q : E_T ⟶ F` from `E_T := (pullback.fst π T.hom)^* E`, such that at every
44	`t : T` the graded Hilbert function of the fibre `F_t` twisted by `L_t`
45	*eventually agrees* with `Φ` (`Scheme.hilbertFunction`; by
46	`Scheme.hilbertPolynomial_eq_of_eventually` this eventual-match encoding is
47	exactly the statement "the Hilbert polynomial of `F|_{X_t}` equals `Φ`" of
48	`def:quot_functor`, and it excludes the junk-value coincidence `Φ = 0`).
49	
50	Two families are equivalent when an isomorphism of the targets commutes with
51	the quotient maps (equivalently, `ker q = ker q'` — same convention as the
52	merged absolute Grassmannian `RankQuotient.Rel`).  The functor value at `T` is
53	the quotient by this relation; the morphism action pulls a family back along
54	the induced morphism on relative products `Scheme.quotBaseMap π ψ` (with the
55	`E`-side matched through the pullback pseudofunctor comparisons
56	`Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp` / `pullbackCongr`).
57	
58	The well-definedness of the pullback action rests on four base-change
59	preservation facts (Nitsure §1: "as properness and flatness are preserved by
60	base-change, and as tensor-product is right exact, the pull-back of
61	`⟨F, q⟩` … is well-defined"):
62	
63	1. surjectivity: pullback is a left adjoint, hence preserves epimorphisms
64	   (proved inline);
65	2. finite presentation: `Scheme.Modules.pullback_isFinitePresentation`
66	   (right-exactness of pullback; the per-slice transport of
67	   `Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean` with finiteness tracking);
68	3. flatness: `Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat.of_isPullback` (Stacks 01U9 lifted to
69	   the sheaf predicate);
70	4. proper support: `Scheme.Modules.HasProperSupport.of_isPullback`
71	   (properness is stable under base change; the schematic support of the
72	   pullback closed-immerses into the base change of the schematic support);
73	5. fibrewise Hilbert polynomials: `Scheme.hilbertFunction_quotBaseMap`
74	   (the fibre of `X_{T'} → T'` at `t'` is the base change of the fibre of
75	   `X_T → T` at `ψ(t')` along the residue extension `κ(ψ t') → κ(t')`, and
76	   `H⁰` of a module with proper support is invariant under base field
77	   extension — flat base change over a field).
78	
79	Items 3–5 are recorded as named typed `sorry` leaves (their statements are the
80	honest Stacks/Nitsure facts; see the blueprint nodes for sources); items 1–2
81	are proved here.  The functor identities reduce to the pseudofunctor
82	coherence laws of `Scheme.Modules.pullback`
83	(`pseudofunctor_right_unitality` / `pseudofunctor_associativity`), packaged
84	once as the app-level lemmas `Scheme.Modules.pullback_id_app_coherence` and
85	`Scheme.Modules.pullback_comp_app_coherence` and consumed by both functors.
86	
87	## References
88	
89	Blueprint: `def:quot_functor`, `def:grassmannian_scheme`,
90	`thm:grassmannian_representable`, `thm:quot_representable`
91	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`).
92	Source: [Nitsure], §1 (FGA Explained Ch. 5, arXiv:math/0504020).
93	-/

97	universe u u₁ u₂ v₁ v₂
98	
99	open CategoryTheory Limits
100	
101	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
102	
103	namespace Scheme
104	
105	/-! ## §0. App-level pseudofunctor coherence for the module pullback
106	
107	Mathlib provides the pseudofunctor coherence laws for
108	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` at the natural-transformation level
109	(`Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_right_unitality`,
110	`Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity`).  The functor laws of the Quot

330	presentation can be read off the transport chain by instance search
331	(every step is `Presentation.map` or `Presentation.ofIsIso`). -/
332	noncomputable def Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation {Y X : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
333	    (F : X.Modules) (A : X.Opens) (P : (F.over A).Presentation) :
334	    (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)).Presentation :=
335	  letI P1 : (F.restrict A.ι).Presentation := presentationRestrictOfOver A F A P le_rfl
336	  letI gA : (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).toScheme ⟶ A.toScheme := g.resLE A (g ⁻¹ᵁ A) le_rfl
337	  haveI hpc : Limits.PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u, u, u, u + 1, u + 1}
338	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback gA) := inferInstance
339	  letI P2 : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback gA).obj (F.restrict A.ι)).Presentation :=
340	    @SheafOfModules.Presentation.map _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ P1
341	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback gA) hpc (pullbackUnitIso gA).symm
342	  letI e : (Scheme.Modules.pullback gA).obj (F.restrict A.ι) ≅
343	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).restrict (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).ι :=
344	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback gA).mapIso
345	        ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback A.ι).app F) ≪≫
346	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp gA A.ι).app F ≪≫
347	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι g le_rfl)).app F ≪≫
348	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).ι g).app F).symm ≪≫
349	      ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).ι).app
350	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F)).symm
351	  letI P5 : (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).restrict (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).ι).Presentation :=
352	    SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u, u, u} e.hom P2
353	  letI eV := modulesOverOpensEquivalence (X := Y) (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)
354	  letI P6 : (eV.inverse.obj (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ A))).Presentation :=
355	    SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u, u, u}
356	      (overOpensIsoRestrict (g ⁻¹ᵁ A) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F)).symm.hom P5
357	  letI ηV : eV.functor.obj (SheafOfModules.unit (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).toScheme.ringCatSheaf) ≅
358	      SheafOfModules.unit (Sheaf.over Y.ringCatSheaf (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)) :=
359	    overOpensFunctorUnitIso (X := Y) (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)
360	  letI P7 : (eV.functor.obj (eV.inverse.obj
361	      (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)))).Presentation :=
362	    P6.map eV.functor ηV.symm
363	  SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u, u, u}
364	    (eV.counitIso.app (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ A))).hom P7
365	
366	/-- The per-slice pullback presentation transport preserves finiteness: every
367	step of `Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation` (and of the underlying
368	`presentationRestrictOfOver` / `presentationOverOpens` transports) is a
369	`Presentation.map` or a `Presentation.ofIsIso`, both of which preserve the
370	generator and relation index types (definitionally — `Presentation.map` via
371	`presentationOfIsCokernelFree` keeps `generators.I`/`relations.I`, and
372	`Presentation.ofIsIso` transports along `GeneratingSections.ofEpi`, which
373	keeps the index).  The `whnf`-reduction certifying this definitional identity
374	exceeds practical heartbeat budgets through the eight-layer transport chain,
375	so the finiteness transfer is recorded as a typed `sorry` (a mechanical
376	index-bookkeeping fact, no mathematical content). -/
377	lemma Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation_isFinite {Y X : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
378	    (F : X.Modules) (A : X.Opens) (P : (F.over A).Presentation) [P.IsFinite] :
379	    (Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation g F A P).IsFinite := by
380	  sorry
381	
382	/-- **Pullback preserves finite presentation** (Stacks 01BK-adjacent; the
383	finite-presentation refinement of `pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom`).  The
384	per-slice transport of `Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean` preserves the
385	index types of the presentations, so the pullback of a finitely presented
386	sheaf of modules along an arbitrary scheme morphism is finitely presented. -/
387	theorem Modules.pullback_isFinitePresentation {Y X : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
388	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsFinitePresentation) :
389	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).IsFinitePresentation := by
390	  obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ := hF.exists_quasicoherentData
391	  have hcov : (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).CoversTop (fun i => g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i) := by
392	    intro W x hx
393	    obtain ⟨U', fU, hf, hU'⟩ := q.coversTop ⊤ (g.base x) (by trivial)
394	    obtain ⟨i, ⟨gi⟩⟩ := hf
395	    exact ⟨W ⊓ (g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i), homOfLE inf_le_left, ⟨i, ⟨homOfLE inf_le_right⟩⟩,
396	      ⟨hx, (leOfHom gi) hU'⟩⟩
397	  let q' : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).QuasicoherentData :=
398	    { I := q.I
399	      X := fun i => g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i
400	      coversTop := hcov
401	      presentation := fun i => Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation g F (q.X i) (q.presentation i) }
402	  have hfin : q'.shrink.IsFinitePresentation := by
403	    apply SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData.IsFinitePresentation.mk
404	    intro i
405	    exact Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation_isFinite g F _ _
406	  exact { exists_quasicoherentData := ⟨q'.shrink, hfin⟩ }
407	
408	/-- **Flatness over the base is stable under base change** (Stacks 01U9,
409	lifted to the coherent-sheaf flatness predicate `Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat`
410	along a cartesian square).  Affine-locally this is `Module.Flat.baseChange`
411	threaded through the quasi-coherent section calculus: the stalk of `g'^* F`
412	is the base change of the stalk of `F`, and the affine-pair predicate is
413	stalk-local for quasi-coherent modules (Stacks 00HT). -/
414	theorem CoherentSheafFlat.of_isPullback
415	    {X S X' S' : Scheme.{u}} {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
416	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g) (F : X.Modules) (hqc : F.IsQuasicoherent)
417	    (hF : CoherentSheafFlat f F) :
418	    CoherentSheafFlat f' ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F) := by
419	  sorry
420	
421	/-- **Proper support is stable under base change** (Nitsure §1; Stacks 01W4 +
422	056H).  For a finitely presented `F`, the annihilator of `g'^* F` contains
423	the pullback of the annihilator of `F`, so the schematic support of `g'^* F`
424	factors through the base change of the schematic support of `F` by a closed
425	immersion; properness is stable under base change and under precomposition
426	with closed immersions. -/
427	theorem Modules.HasProperSupport.of_isPullback
428	    {X S X' S' : Scheme.{u}} {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
429	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g) (F : X.Modules) (hfp : F.IsFinitePresentation)

105	/-! ## §0. App-level pseudofunctor coherence for the module pullback
106	
107	Mathlib provides the pseudofunctor coherence laws for
108	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` at the natural-transformation level
109	(`Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_right_unitality`,
110	`Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity`).  The functor laws of the Quot
111	and Grassmannian functors need them in *component* form, conjugated by the
112	`eqToIso`-valued comparison `Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr` along the triangle
113	identities of the relevant scheme morphisms.  We package the two required
114	shapes once. -/
115	
116	namespace Modules
117	
118	/-- Component form of `Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr`: it is an `eqToHom`. -/
119	lemma pullbackCongr_hom_app {Y X : Scheme.{u}} {f g : Y ⟶ X} (h : f = g)
120	    (M : X.Modules) :
121	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h).hom.app M = eqToHom (by rw [h]) := by
122	  subst h
123	  simp [Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr]
124	
125	/-- Component form of `Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr`, inverse direction. -/
126	lemma pullbackCongr_inv_app {Y X : Scheme.{u}} {f g : Y ⟶ X} (h : f = g)
127	    (M : X.Modules) :
128	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h).inv.app M = eqToHom (by rw [h]) := by
129	  subst h
130	  simp [Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr]

1381	namespace Scheme
1382	
1383	/-! ## §1. Coherent-sheaf flatness over a base
1384	
1385	For a morphism `f : X ⟶ S` of schemes and a coherent (more generally:
1386	quasi-coherent) `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, the *coherent-sheaf flatness* predicate
1387	`CoherentSheafFlat f 𝓕` captures Stacks 00HB lifted to the sheaf level:
1388	for every point `x ∈ X` the stalk `𝓕_x` is a flat `𝓞_{S, f(x)}`-module
1389	via the composite `𝓞_{S, f(x)} → 𝓞_{X, x} → End(𝓕_x)`.
1390	
1391	Affine-locally (Stacks 00HB equivalence): on an affine open `U = Spec A
1392	⊆ S` and an affine open `V = Spec B ⊆ f⁻¹U` with `𝓕|_V = M̃` for a
1393	finite `B`-module `M`, the condition becomes `Module.Flat A M` with the
1394	`A`-module structure inherited via the ring map `A → B`. The
1395	file-internal encoding uses this affine-local form.
1396	
1397	Blueprint reference: `def:coherent_sheaf_flat`. -/
1398	
1399	/-- A coherent sheaf `𝓕` on `X` is **flat over `S`** for a morphism
1400	`f : X ⟶ S` if, for every affine open `U ⊆ S` and every affine open
1401	`V ⊆ f⁻¹U`, the `𝓞_X`-module of sections `Γ(𝓕, V)` is flat as a
1402	`Γ(S, U)`-module, where the `Γ(S, U)`-action is the restriction of
1403	scalars along the ring morphism `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V)` (the `appLE`
1404	morphism of `f`).
1405	
1406	This is the affine-local form of Stacks 00HB (a coherent sheaf is flat
1407	over the base if every stalk is flat over the corresponding base
1408	stalk); the affine-local form and stalk form are equivalent for
1409	*quasi-coherent* sheaves on a noetherian base, which is the regime of
1410	the flattening-stratification theorem.
1411	
1412	iter-177+: the body of subsequent flat-locus theorems may package this
1413	predicate as an instance-driven typeclass `Module.Flat`-style if the
1414	sheafified-tensor infrastructure lands in Mathlib; for the iter-176
1415	file-skeleton it is encoded as a `Prop` quantifier. -/
1416	def CoherentSheafFlat {X S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
1417	  ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) {V : X.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
1418	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
1419	    letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V) := Module.compHom _ (f.appLE U V e).hom
1420	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V)
1421	
1422	end Scheme

3410	    {R S N : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
3411	    [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [Module S N] [IsScalarTower R S N]

3434	of modules `M` on a scheme `X` and a *quasi-compact, quasi-separated* open `W ⊆ X`, the
3435	section restriction `Γ(M, W) → Γ(M, D(g))` at any `g : Γ(X, W)` exhibits the target as the
3436	localization `Γ(M, W)[1/g]` over `Γ(X, W)`. `map_units` holds because `g` restricts to a
3437	unit of `Γ(X, D(g))` (`RingedSpace.isUnit_res_basicOpen`); `surj`/`exists_of_eq` are the
3438	Mayer–Vietoris induction engines `exists_res_eq_pow_smul_of_isCompact` /

3441	theorem isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isCompact
3442	    (M : X.Modules) [M.IsQuasicoherent] {W : X.Opens}
3443	    (hW : IsCompact (W : Set X)) (hsep : IsQuasiSeparated (W : Set X))
3444	    (g : Γ(X, W))
3445	    [Module Γ(X, W) Γ(M, X.basicOpen g)]
3446	    [IsScalarTower Γ(X, W) Γ(X, X.basicOpen g) Γ(M, X.basicOpen g)] :
3447	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) (restrictBasicOpenₗ M g) where
3448	  map_units s := by
3449	    obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := s.2
3450	    have hu : IsUnit (algebraMap Γ(X, W) Γ(X, X.basicOpen g) (s : Γ(X, W))) := by
3451	      rw [← hk, map_pow]
3452	      exact (X.toLocallyRingedSpace.toRingedSpace.isUnit_res_basicOpen g).pow k
3453	    exact isUnit_algebraMap_end_of_isUnit_algebraMap hu
3454	  surj y := by
3455	    have hDW : X.basicOpen g ≤ W ⊓ X.basicOpen g := le_inf (X.basicOpen_le g) le_rfl
3456	    obtain ⟨x, k, hx⟩ := exists_res_eq_pow_smul_of_isCompact M g hsep W hW le_rfl
3457	      (M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (inf_le_right : W ⊓ X.basicOpen g ≤ X.basicOpen g)).op y)
3458	    refine ⟨⟨x, ⟨g ^ k, k, rfl⟩⟩, ?_⟩
3459	    have e1 : (g ^ k : Γ(X, W)) • y
3460	        = X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le g)).op g ^ k • y := by
3461	      rw [← algebraMap_smul Γ(X, X.basicOpen g) (g ^ k) y, map_pow]
3462	      rfl
3463	    have e2 := congrArg (M.presheaf.map (homOfLE hDW).op) hx
3464	    rw [res_res M (inf_le_left : W ⊓ X.basicOpen g ≤ W) hDW (X.basicOpen_le g) x,
3465	      map_smul, map_pow,
3466	      resRing_res ((inf_le_left : W ⊓ X.basicOpen g ≤ W).trans le_rfl) hDW
3467	        (X.basicOpen_le g) g,
3468	      res_res M (inf_le_right : W ⊓ X.basicOpen g ≤ X.basicOpen g) hDW le_rfl y,
3469	      res_self M y] at e2
3470	    show (g ^ k : Γ(X, W)) • y = M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le g)).op x
3471	    rw [e1, e2]

3473	    have h' := congrArg
3474	      (M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (inf_le_right : W ⊓ X.basicOpen g ≤ X.basicOpen g)).op) h

3477	      show restrictBasicOpenₗ M g x₂

3480	        (inf_le_left : W ⊓ X.basicOpen g ≤ W) x₁,

3482	        (inf_le_left : W ⊓ X.basicOpen g ≤ W) x₂] at h'

3490	/-- **Per-affine coherence of the annihilator family** (the `map_ideal_basicOpen` content for
3491	`def:modules_annihilator`, `lem:modules_annihilator_ideal`). For a quasi-coherent `F`, an affine
3492	open `V`, and `f : Γ(X, V)` with `Γ(F, V)` finitely generated over `Γ(X, V)`, the module annihilator
3493	`Ann_{Γ(X,V)}(Γ(F,V))` maps, under the basic-open restriction `Γ(X, V) → Γ(X, D(f))`, onto
3494	`Ann_{Γ(X,D(f))}(Γ(F,D(f)))`. This is the localized-annihilator identity
3495	(`Module.annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map`, `lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map`) transported
3496	across the quasi-coherent basic-open section localization (gap2, `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`,
3497	`lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`). It is exactly the structure field
3498	`Scheme.IdealSheafData.map_ideal_basicOpen` for the annihilator data. Project-local because
3499	`annihilator` is. -/
3500	lemma annihilator_map_basicOpen (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
3501	    (V : X.affineOpens) (f : Γ(X, V.1)) [Module.Finite Γ(X, V.1) Γ(F, V.1)] :
3502	    (Module.annihilator Γ(X, V.1) Γ(F, V.1)).map
3503	        (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op).hom
3504	      = Module.annihilator Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) Γ(F, X.basicOpen f) := by
3505	  haveI := V.2.isLocalization_basicOpen f
3506	  letI : Module Γ(X, V.1) Γ(F, X.basicOpen f) :=
3507	    Module.compHom _ (algebraMap Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f))
3508	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) Γ(F, X.basicOpen f) :=
3509	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)
3510	  haveI := isLocalizedModule_basicOpen F V.2 f
3511	  exact (Module.annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map (Submonoid.powers f)
3512	    (restrictBasicOpenₗ F f)).symm
3513	
3514	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
3515	/-- **The annihilator ideal sheaf agrees with the module annihilator on affine opens**
3516	(`lem:modules_annihilator_ideal`). For a quasi-coherent `F` whose section module `Γ(F, V)` is
3517	finitely generated over `Γ(X, V)` on every affine open `V` (the affine-local finiteness supplied,
3518	for a finite-type `F`, by the G1 base case `lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections`), the section over
3519	an affine open `U` of the annihilator ideal sheaf equals the module annihilator of the sections:
3520	`(annihilator F).ideal U = Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))`.
3521	
3522	The forward inclusion `≤` is `annihilator_ideal_le` (always available from `ofIdeals`). The reverse
3523	inclusion is genuinely *global*: `annihilator F = ofIdeals (V ↦ Ann_{Γ(X,V)}(Γ(F,V)))` is the
3524	**largest** ideal sheaf contained in the annihilator family, and its value at `U` reaches the full
3525	`Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` precisely when that family is itself a coherent ideal sheaf — i.e. when it
3526	satisfies `IdealSheafData.map_ideal_basicOpen` at *every* affine open. That coherence is
3527	`annihilator_map_basicOpen`, which needs finite generation of `Γ(F, V)` at each `V`; hence the
3528	`hfin` hypothesis. With it the family assembles into an honest `IdealSheafData I` whose `ideal` is
3529	the annihilator family, and `IdealSheafData.ofIdeals_ideal` gives `ofIdeals I.ideal = I`, whence the
3530	claim at every `U` at once. This mirrors Mathlib's `Scheme.Hom.ker_apply`, which likewise builds the
3531	full ideal sheaf and reads off the affine value (using `QuasiCompact f` as its global hypothesis).
3532	Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
3533	theorem annihilator_ideal (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
3534	    (hfin : ∀ V : X.affineOpens, Module.Finite Γ(X, V.1) Γ(F, V.1)) (U : X.affineOpens) :
3535	    (annihilator F).ideal U = Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1) := by
3536	  let I : X.IdealSheafData :=
3537	    ⟨fun V => Module.annihilator Γ(X, V.1) Γ(F, V.1), ?_, _, rfl⟩
3538	  · exact congr($(IdealSheafData.ofIdeals_ideal I).ideal U)
3539	  · intro V f

---
isomorphism that drives the affine pullback dictionary `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` via
`Adjunction.natIsoOfRightAdjointNatIso`. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def gammaPushforwardNatIso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (φ : R ⟶ R') :
    Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ) ⋙ moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R) ≅
      moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R') ⋙ ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom :=
  NatIso.ofComponents (fun N => gammaPushforwardIso φ N) (by
    intro N N' g
    ext x
    rfl)

/-- **Affine pullback of a tilde-module.** For a ring map `φ : R ⟶ R'` and an `R`-module `M`,
pulling the quasi-coherent sheaf `M^~` back along `Spec φ` is, up to canonical isomorphism, the
tilde of the extension of scalars `R' ⊗_R M`. This is the pullback companion of
`pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` and is part (1) of Stacks Tag 01I9 (`lemma-widetilde-pullback`).

The construction is the uniqueness-of-left-adjoints route. Pullback along `Spec φ` is left adjoint
to pushforward, and `tilde` is left adjoint to global sections; their composite
`tilde_R ⋙ pullback` is left adjoint to `pushforward ⋙ Γ_R`. Symmetrically `extendScalars φ` is
left adjoint to `restrictScalars φ`, so `extendScalars ⋙ tilde_{R'}` is left adjoint to
`Γ_{R'} ⋙ restrictScalars φ`. The two right adjoints are identified by the natural isomorphism
`gammaPushforwardNatIso` (which is the global-sections comparison `gammaPushforwardIso`), so
`Adjunction.natIsoOfRightAdjointNatIso` yields an isomorphism of the two left adjoints; evaluating
at `M` gives the claim. Project-local: the affine companion of the pushforward dictionary. See
blueprint `lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso`. -/
noncomputable def pullback_spec_tilde_iso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}}
    (φ : R ⟶ R') (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M) ≅
      tilde ((ModuleCat.extendScalars φ.hom).obj M) :=
  let adjL := (tilde.adjunction (R := R)).comp
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map φ))
  let adjR := (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj φ.hom).comp (tilde.adjunction (R := R'))
  (((conjugateIsoEquiv adjL adjR).symm (gammaPushforwardNatIso φ)).symm).app M

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — affine termwise base-change brick (IN PROGRESS)

The concrete-tilde route to flat base change (Stacks 02KH) builds a *fresh* base-change
isomorphism `e : g^* ∘ f_* ≅ f'_* ∘ (g')^*` by gluing affine-local isomorphisms, instead of
proving the canonical adjoint mate `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is an isomorphism (which walls on the
mate ↔ `cancelBaseChange` coherence). The affine-local brick is the floor of that construction:
over the fully affine pushout square
```
  Spec (A ⊗_R R') --Spec ρ--> Spec A
  |Spec σ                      |Spec φ
  v                            v
  Spec R'      ----Spec ψ---->  Spec R
```
(`φ : R → A`, `ψ : R → R'`, `ρ = includeLeft`, `σ = includeRight` into `A ⊗_R R'`) it produces the
termwise isomorphism `g^*(f_* M̃) ≅ f'_*((g')^* M̃)`, assembled from the two sorry-free tilde
dictionaries `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` / `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` and the pure
commutative-algebra cancellation `Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange`, never forming the adjoint
mate.

STATUS (iter-305): the load-bearing module-level core — the `R'`-linear iso
`(extendScalars ψ) ∘ (restrictScalars φ) ≅ (restrictScalars σ) ∘ (extendScalars ρ)` realising
`R' ⊗_R M ≅ (A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M` — was PROVEN to typecheck axiom-clean in the **CommRingCat framing**
(ambient algebras supplied as `φ.hom.toAlgebra` / `ψ.hom.toAlgebra`), by `@`-threading the
`RingHom.toAlgebra` instances through `Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange` (so its output is defeq to
the `compHom`-based `ModuleCat.extendScalars`/`restrictScalars` objects) with `IsPushout` transported
from `TensorProduct.isPushout'` via `Algebra.algebra_ext`. See `task_results` for the full working
construction. It is NOT yet landed as a top-level declaration: stating the tensor `A ⊗_R R'` in the
signature forces a Type-u framing whose *opaque* ambient `[Algebra R A]`/`[Algebra R R']` lets the
GLOBAL `Algebra.TensorProduct.leftAlgebra`/`rightAlgebra` instances win over the `toAlgebra` `letI`s,
breaking the `compHom`-defeq on the inner `Module A (A⊗_R R')` and outer `Module R' (·)`. The
recommended fix (iter-306) is the abstract-`B` framing: take `ρ : A ⟶ B`, `σ : R' ⟶ B` and a
`CategoryTheory.IsPushout φ ψ ρ σ` (or `IsPullback` of the Spec square) hypothesis, derive
`Algebra.IsPushout` in the proof with all algebras `toAlgebra` (no global tensor instances to shadow),
and assemble the 5-step chain. See blueprint `lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange`. -/

/-- **Module-level base-change cancellation iso (abstract-`B` framing).** Given a pushout square of
commutative rings
```
  R --φ--> A
  |ψ       |ρ
  v        v
  R' --σ-> B
```
(so `B ≅ A ⊗_R R'`) and an `A`-module `M`, the `R'`-linear isomorphism
`extendScalars_ψ (restrictScalars_φ M) ≅ restrictScalars_σ (extendScalars_ρ M)`, i.e.
`R' ⊗_R M ≅ restrictScalars_σ (B ⊗_A M)`. It realises the inverse of Mathlib's
`Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange` between the extension-of-scalars objects attached to the two
legs of the square. The abstract `B` (rather than the concrete `A ⊗_R R'`) keeps all ambient
algebra instances `toAlgebra`, so no global tensor-product instance shadows them and
`cancelBaseChange`'s output module structures land defeq on the `compHom`-based
`extendScalars`/`restrictScalars` objects. Project-local: the module core of the affine termwise
base-change brick. See blueprint `lem:baseChangeCancelModuleIso`. -/
noncomputable def baseChangeCancelModuleIso {R A R' B : CommRingCat.{u}}
    (φ : R ⟶ A) (ψ : R ⟶ R') (ρ : A ⟶ B) (σ : R' ⟶ B)
    (h : CategoryTheory.IsPushout φ ψ ρ σ) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
    (ModuleCat.extendScalars ψ.hom).obj ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M) ≅
      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars σ.hom).obj ((ModuleCat.extendScalars ρ.hom).obj M) := by
  letI iRA : Algebra (R : Type u) (A : Type u) := φ.hom.toAlgebra
  letI iRR' : Algebra (R : Type u) (R' : Type u) := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
  letI iAB : Algebra (A : Type u) (B : Type u) := ρ.hom.toAlgebra
  letI iR'B : Algebra (R' : Type u) (B : Type u) := σ.hom.toAlgebra
  letI iRB : Algebra (R : Type u) (B : Type u) := (ρ.hom.comp φ.hom).toAlgebra
  letI iMR : Module (R : Type u) M := ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M).isModule
  haveI tRAM : IsScalarTower (R : Type u) A M :=
    ⟨fun r a m => by rw [Algebra.smul_def, mul_smul]; rfl⟩
  haveI tRAB : @IsScalarTower (R : Type u) A B _ iAB.toSMul iRB.toSMul :=
    @IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq (R : Type u) A B _ _ _ iRA iAB iRB (fun _ => rfl)
  haveI tRR'B : @IsScalarTower (R : Type u) R' B _ iR'B.toSMul iRB.toSMul :=

/-

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.PullbackQuasicoherent

/-!
# Pullback preserves finite presentation (Stacks 01BK-adjacent)

The finite-presentation refinement of the arbitrary-morphism pullback
quasi-coherence of `Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean` (Stacks 01BG): the
pullback of a finitely presented sheaf of modules along an arbitrary scheme
morphism is finitely presented (`Scheme.Modules.pullback_isFinitePresentation`,
blueprint `lem:pullback_finite_presentation`).

## Finiteness bookkeeping

Every step of the per-slice presentation transports
(`presentationOverOpens`, `presentationRestrictOfOver` of
`Cohomology/CechTermAcyclic.lean`, and `Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation`
below) is a `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` along a colimit-preserving
functor or a `SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso`, both of which keep the
generator and relation index types.  Mathlib supplies the `IsFinite` instance
for `Presentation.ofIsIso`; this file supplies the missing companion instance
for `Presentation.map` (the index types are preserved *definitionally*, by
iota-reduction through `presentationOfIsCokernelFree`), together with
`IsFinite` instances for the two project transports.  With one instance per
transport layer, instance search closes the finiteness of the eight-layer
chain by structural head-matching — no `whnf` through the heavy transport
terms is ever attempted (the earlier `inferInstanceAs`-on-the-unexpanded-`def`
approach exceeded 8M heartbeats and was abandoned; see the session notes on
`pullbackSlicePresentation_isFinite`).

## References

Blueprint: `lem:pullback_finite_presentation`, `def:quot_functor`

set_option autoImplicit false

universe u u₁ u₂ v₁ v₂

open CategoryTheory Limits

namespace SheafOfModules

variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
  {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
  [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat]
  [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)]
variable {C' : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} C'] {J' : GrothendieckTopology C'}
  {S : Sheaf J' RingCat.{u}}
  [HasSheafify J' AddCommGrpCat] [J'.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat]
  [J'.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)]

/-- Mapping a presentation along a colimit-preserving functor preserves
finiteness: `Presentation.map` is `presentationOfIsCokernelFree` at the mapped
generator/relation data, which keeps the index types `generators.I` and
`relations.I` definitionally (Mathlib supplies the analogous instance for
`Presentation.ofIsIso`; this is the missing `map` companion). -/
instance Presentation.map_isFinite {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (P : Presentation M)
    (F : SheafOfModules.{u} R ⥤ SheafOfModules.{u} S)
    [PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u} F] (η : unit S ≅ F.obj (unit R))
    [P.IsFinite] : (P.map F η).IsFinite where
  isFiniteType_generators := ⟨inferInstanceAs (Finite P.generators.I)⟩
  isFiniteType_relations := ⟨inferInstanceAs (Finite P.relations.I)⟩

end SheafOfModules

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

/-- The over-restriction transport of presentations preserves finiteness:
`presentationOverOpens` is a chain of two `Presentation.map`s and one
`Presentation.ofIsIso`. -/
instance presentationOverOpens_isFinite {X : Scheme.{u}} (W : X.Opens)
    (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens) (P : (M.over U).Presentation) (hWU : W ≤ U)
    [P.IsFinite] : (presentationOverOpens W M U P hWU).IsFinite := by
  delta presentationOverOpens
  infer_instance

/-- The restriction-from-over transport of presentations preserves finiteness:
`presentationRestrictOfOver` is `presentationOverOpens` followed by a
`Presentation.map` and a `Presentation.ofIsIso`. -/
instance presentationRestrictOfOver_isFinite {X : Scheme.{u}} (W : X.Opens)
    (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens) (P : (M.over U).Presentation) (hWU : W ≤ U)
    [P.IsFinite] : (presentationRestrictOfOver W M U P hWU).IsFinite := by
  delta presentationRestrictOfOver
  infer_instance

namespace Scheme

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 1000000 in
set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
/-- Term-mode variant of `presentationPullbackSliceOfOver`
(`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`) — the same per-slice transport of a
presentation of `F.over A` to a presentation of `(g^* F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)`,
restated without tactic-`set` wrappers so that the finiteness of the
presentation can be read off the transport chain by instance search
(every step is `Presentation.map` or `Presentation.ofIsIso`). -/
noncomputable def Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation {Y X : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
    (F : X.Modules) (A : X.Opens) (P : (F.over A).Presentation) :
    (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)).Presentation :=
  letI P1 : (F.restrict A.ι).Presentation := presentationRestrictOfOver A F A P le_rfl
  letI gA : (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).toScheme ⟶ A.toScheme := g.resLE A (g ⁻¹ᵁ A) le_rfl
  haveI hpc : Limits.PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u, u, u, u + 1, u + 1}
      (Scheme.Modules.pullback gA) := inferInstance
  letI P2 : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback gA).obj (F.restrict A.ι)).Presentation :=
    @SheafOfModules.Presentation.map _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ P1
      (Scheme.Modules.pullback gA) hpc (pullbackUnitIso gA).symm
  letI e : (Scheme.Modules.pullback gA).obj (F.restrict A.ι) ≅
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).restrict (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).ι :=
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback gA).mapIso
        ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback A.ι).app F) ≪≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp gA A.ι).app F ≪≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι g le_rfl)).app F ≪≫
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).ι g).app F).symm ≪≫
      ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).ι).app
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F)).symm
  letI P5 : (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).restrict (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).ι).Presentation :=
    SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u, u, u} e.hom P2
  letI eV := modulesOverOpensEquivalence (X := Y) (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)
  letI P6 : (eV.inverse.obj (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ A))).Presentation :=
    SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u, u, u}
      (overOpensIsoRestrict (g ⁻¹ᵁ A) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F)).symm.hom P5
  letI ηV : eV.functor.obj (SheafOfModules.unit (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).toScheme.ringCatSheaf) ≅
      SheafOfModules.unit (Sheaf.over Y.ringCatSheaf (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)) :=
    overOpensFunctorUnitIso (X := Y) (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)
  letI P7 : (eV.functor.obj (eV.inverse.obj
      (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)))).Presentation :=
    P6.map eV.functor ηV.symm
  SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u, u, u}
    (eV.counitIso.app (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ A))).hom P7

set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 1000000 in
set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
/-- The per-slice pullback presentation transport preserves finiteness: after
`delta`-exposing the transport chain, every layer is a `Presentation.map` or a
`Presentation.ofIsIso`, and the per-layer `IsFinite` instances close the goal
by structural head-matching (`lem:pullback_finite_presentation`). -/
lemma Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation_isFinite {Y X : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
    (F : X.Modules) (A : X.Opens) (P : (F.over A).Presentation) [P.IsFinite] :
    (Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation g F A P).IsFinite := by
  delta Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation
  infer_instance

/-- **Pullback preserves finite presentation** (Stacks 01BK-adjacent; the
finite-presentation refinement of `pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom`).  The
per-slice transport of `Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean` preserves the
index types of the presentations, so the pullback of a finitely presented
sheaf of modules along an arbitrary scheme morphism is finitely presented. -/
theorem Modules.pullback_isFinitePresentation {Y X : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsFinitePresentation) :
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).IsFinitePresentation := by
  obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ := hF.exists_quasicoherentData
  have hcov : (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).CoversTop (fun i => g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i) := by
    intro W x hx
    obtain ⟨U', fU, hf, hU'⟩ := q.coversTop ⊤ (g.base x) (by trivial)
    obtain ⟨i, ⟨gi⟩⟩ := hf
    exact ⟨W ⊓ (g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i), homOfLE inf_le_left, ⟨i, ⟨homOfLE inf_le_right⟩⟩,
      ⟨hx, (leOfHom gi) hU'⟩⟩
  let q' : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).QuasicoherentData :=
    { I := q.I
      X := fun i => g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i
      coversTop := hcov
      presentation := fun i => Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation g F (q.X i) (q.presentation i) }
  have hfin : q'.shrink.IsFinitePresentation := by
    apply SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData.IsFinitePresentation.mk
    intro i
    exact Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation_isFinite g F _ _
  exact { exists_quasicoherentData := ⟨q'.shrink, hfin⟩ }

end Scheme

end AlgebraicGeometry

4665:  -- Step 1 (Stacks 01I8 — `tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen`):
===
quasi-coherent `F : X.Modules`, the restriction `(V.ι)^* F` is quasi-coherent on `V`.  This is the
open-immersion case the fibre-power projections of the {\v C}ech nerve require (each
`Y_n = U_{i₀} ∩ ⋯ ∩ U_{iₙ} ↪ X` is an open immersion); the general-morphism case is
`pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom` (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`), which localizes the
pullback along the preimage cover before transporting the presentation.  A thin re-export of
`isQuasicoherent_pullback_opens` (proved via `IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop` on the preimage cover).
Project-local; blueprint `lem:pullback_preserves_quasicoherent`. -/
theorem pullback_isQuasicoherent (V : X.Opens) (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) :
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).obj F).IsQuasicoherent :=
  isQuasicoherent_pullback_opens V F hF

/-- **Altitude 1 of the bridge: `(V.ι)^* F` pushed to `Spec Γ(X,V)` is `tilde N`** (Stacks 01I8).
For a quasi-coherent `F : X.Modules` and an affine open `V` of `X`, the restriction `(V.ι)^* F`,
pushed forward along the whole-scheme iso `isoSpec : V ≅ Spec Γ(X, V)`, is canonically isomorphic to
the `tilde` of its module of global sections `N = Γ(Spec Γ(X,V), -)`.  The pullback is quasi-coherent
(`pullback_isQuasicoherent`) and quasi-coherence is preserved by the iso-pushforward
(`pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh`), so the unconditional affine structure theorem 01I8
(`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`, via the live instance `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_quasicoherent`) applies.
Project-local; blueprint `lem:pushPullObj_iso_tilde` (altitude 1). -/
noncomputable def pullbackRestrict_iso_tilde (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent)
    {V : X.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward hV.isoSpec.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).obj F) ≅
      tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj
        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward hV.isoSpec.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).obj F))) :=
  haveI : ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward hV.isoSpec.hom).obj
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).obj F)).IsQuasicoherent :=
    pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh hV.isoSpec ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).obj F)
      (pullback_isQuasicoherent V F hF)
  qcoh_iso_tilde_sections _

/-- **Altitude 2 of the bridge: `f_*(p_* p^* F) ≅ (Spec φ)_* (tilde N)`** (Stacks 01I8, assembled
pushed-forward level).  Over the affine base `S = Spec R`, with `V` an affine open of `X` and
`φ := Spec.preimage (fromSpec ≫ f) : R ⟶ Γ(X, V)` the ring map presenting the composite
`isoSpec.inv ≫ V.ι ≫ f = fromSpec ≫ f` as `Spec.map φ`, the pushed-forward push–pull object
`(pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.ι))` is canonically isomorphic to
`(pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde N)`.

The construction: collapse `f_* ∘ (V.ι)_*` to `(V.ι ≫ f)_*` by `pushforwardComp`; rewrite
`V.ι ≫ f = isoSpec.hom ≫ Spec.map φ` (from `Spec.map_preimage` and `isoSpec_inv_ι`) by
`pushforwardCongr`; split off `(Spec.map φ)_*` by `pushforwardComp` again (leaving the altitude-1
domain `(isoSpec.hom)_* ((V.ι)^* F)`); then push altitude 1 (`pullbackRestrict_iso_tilde`) through
`(Spec.map φ)_*`.  The right-hand side is exactly the form consumed by the brick
`cech_degree_affine_baseChange`.  Project-local; blueprint `lem:pushPullObj_iso_tilde` (altitude 2). -/
noncomputable def pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
    (f : X ⟶ Spec R) (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent)
    {V : X.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.ι)) ≅
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map (Spec.preimage (hV.fromSpec ≫ f)))).obj
        (tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj
          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward hV.isoSpec.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).obj F)))) :=
  have heq : V.ι ≫ f = hV.isoSpec.hom ≫ Spec.map (Spec.preimage (hV.fromSpec ≫ f)) := by
    rw [Spec.map_preimage, ← IsAffineOpen.isoSpec_inv_ι hV, Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
  (pushforwardComp V.ι f).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).obj F) ≪≫
    (pushforwardCongr heq).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).obj F) ≪≫
    (pushforwardComp hV.isoSpec.hom (Spec.map (Spec.preimage (hV.fromSpec ≫ f)))).symm.app
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).obj F) ≪≫
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map (Spec.preimage (hV.fromSpec ≫ f)))).mapIso
      (pullbackRestrict_iso_tilde F hF hV)

/-- **Altitude 2 over an abstract affine base** (Stacks 01I8, abstract-`S` generalization of
`pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde`).  For a *separated* `f : X ⟶ S` with `S` an **abstract** affine
scheme (`[IsAffine S]`, so `S` need not be a literal `Spec`), write `e_S := S.isoSpec : S ≅ Spec Γ(S)`
for the canonical affine identification.  The pushed-forward push–pull object
`(pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.ι))` is canonically isomorphic to the altitude-2
`(Spec φ)_*(tilde N)` form, **transported back along `e_S⁻¹`** so it lands in `O_S`-modules rather
than `O_{Spec Γ(S)}`-modules, where `φ := Spec.preimage (hV.fromSpec ≫ (f ≫ e_S.hom))` presents
`(e_S ∘ f) ∘ (isoSpec ∘ j_V)` as `Spec φ`.

Construction (≤10 lines): the composite `f ≫ e_S.hom : X ⟶ Spec Γ(S)` has a *literal* affine base,
so the literal-Spec bridge `pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde` applies to it; then conjugate by

2070	    have hcc := congrArg e₁ hc'
2071	    rw [he₁, he₁, e₁.apply_symm_apply, e₁.apply_symm_apply] at hcc
2072	    exact hcc

2074	/-- **(II) Localization at a base-changed submonoid descends to the base ring.** If `g` is
2075	`Rr`-linear and exhibits a localization at `powers (algebraMap R Rr f)` over a base-changed ring
2076	`Rr` (an algebra over `R` — here `R` localized at some `r`), then its restriction of scalars to `R`
2077	is a localization
2078	at `powers f` over `R`. This lets the `R_r`-level localization that P1 (`IsIso fromTildeΓ`) produces
2079	on the slice `Spec R_r` be read back as the `R`-level `Hfr` data the cover-form descent consumes.
2080	Mathlib-absent; project-local. -/
2081	theorem isLocalizedModule_restrictScalars_powers_algebraMap
2082	    {R Rr : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing Rr] [Algebra R Rr]
2083	    {M₁ M₂ : Type*} [AddCommGroup M₁] [Module R M₁] [Module Rr M₁] [IsScalarTower R Rr M₁]
2084	    [AddCommGroup M₂] [Module R M₂] [Module Rr M₂] [IsScalarTower R Rr M₂]
2085	    (f : R) (g : M₁ →ₗ[Rr] M₂)
2086	    [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R Rr f)) g] :
2087	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (g.restrictScalars R) where
2088	  map_units x := by
2089	    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := x.2
2090	    have hn' : f ^ n = ↑x := hn
2091	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
2092	    have hmem : (algebraMap R Rr f) ^ n ∈ Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R Rr f) := ⟨n, rfl⟩
2093	    have hsrc := IsLocalizedModule.map_units g ⟨_, hmem⟩
2094	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff] at hsrc
2095	    have hfun : ⇑(algebraMap R (Module.End R M₂) (↑x : R))
2096	        = ⇑(algebraMap Rr (Module.End Rr M₂) ((algebraMap R Rr f) ^ n)) := by
2097	      funext z
2098	      rw [Module.algebraMap_end_apply, Module.algebraMap_end_apply, ← hn', ← map_pow,
2099	        algebraMap_smul]
2100	    rw [hfun]; exact hsrc
2101	  surj y := by
2102	    obtain ⟨⟨x, s⟩, hx⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R Rr f)) g y
2103	    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := s.2
2104	    have hn' : (algebraMap R Rr f) ^ n = ↑s := hn
2105	    refine ⟨⟨x, ⟨f ^ n, n, rfl⟩⟩, ?_⟩
2106	    have hsmul : (f ^ n : R) • y = (↑s : Rr) • y := by
2107	      rw [← hn', ← map_pow, algebraMap_smul]
2108	    change (f ^ n : R) • y = (g.restrictScalars R) x
2109	    rw [hsmul, LinearMap.coe_restrictScalars]; exact hx
2110	  exists_of_eq {x₁ x₂} heq := by
2111	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq (S := Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R Rr f))
2112	      (f := g) heq
2113	    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := c.2
2114	    have hn' : (algebraMap R Rr f) ^ n = ↑c := hn
2115	    refine ⟨⟨f ^ n, n, rfl⟩, ?_⟩
2116	    have e1 : (f ^ n : R) • x₁ = (↑c : Rr) • x₁ := by rw [← hn', ← map_pow, algebraMap_smul]
2117	    have e2 : (f ^ n : R) • x₂ = (↑c : Rr) • x₂ := by rw [← hn', ← map_pow, algebraMap_smul]
2118	    change (f ^ n : R) • x₁ = (f ^ n : R) • x₂
2119	    rw [e1, e2]; exact hc

2121	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — gap1-D Hfr: pullback-section transport
2122	
2123	The remaining gated ingredient of the named gap1 keystone
2124	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` is the **section-level** analogue of P1's object-level
2125	transport: for an open immersion `f : X ⟶ Y` and a sheaf of modules `M` on `Y`, the sections of the
2126	geometric pullback `(pullback f).obj M` over an open `U ⊆ X` are canonically identified with the
2127	sections of `M` over the image `f ''ᵁ U`.
2128	
2129	The construction is `Γ(-, U)` applied to the inverse of Mathlib's `restrictFunctorIsoPullback f`
2130	(`restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f`) at `M`, using the *definitional* identity
2131	`Γ((restrictFunctor f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)` (`Scheme.Modules.restrict_obj`, `rfl`). Because
2132	both sides are `Γ(-, U)` of a single fixed module isomorphism, naturality in `U` (intertwining the
2133	presheaf restriction maps) is free: it is the naturality of the underlying abelian-presheaf
2134	morphism. Mathlib-absent at the pinned commit; project-bespoke. -/
2135	
2136	/-- **Global sections of a pullback along an open immersion are sections over the image**
2137	(gap1, Hfr section transport, general open). For an open immersion `f : X ⟶ Y`, a sheaf of modules
2138	`M` on `Y`, and an open `U ⊆ X`, the additive groups of sections satisfy
2139	`Γ((pullback f).obj M, U) ≅ Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)`. This is `Γ(-, U)` of `(restrictFunctorIsoPullback f)⁻¹`
2140	at `M`; the codomain is `Γ((restrictFunctor f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)` definitionally. Naturality
2141	in `U` is `gammaPullbackImageIso_hom_naturality`. -/
2142	noncomputable def gammaPullbackImageIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f]
2143	    (M : Y.Modules) (U : X.Opens) :
2144	    Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M, U) ≅ Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U) :=
2145	  (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X ⋙ (CategoryTheory.evaluation _ _).obj (Opposite.op U)).mapIso
2146	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).symm.app M)
2147	
2148	/-- **The pullback-section comparison intertwines the restriction maps** (gap1, Hfr, naturality).
2149	For opens `V ≤ U` of `X`, `gammaPullbackImageIso` commutes with the presheaf restriction maps of
2150	`(pullback f).obj M` and of `M` (along the image inclusion `f ''ᵁ V ≤ f ''ᵁ U`). This is the
2151	naturality of the underlying morphism of abelian presheaves. -/
2152	theorem gammaPullbackImageIso_hom_naturality {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f]
2153	    (M : Y.Modules) {U V : X.Opens} (i : V ⟶ U) :
2154	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M).presheaf.map i.op ≫ (gammaPullbackImageIso f M V).hom
2155	      = (gammaPullbackImageIso f M U).hom ≫ M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op := by
2156	  exact (((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).symm.app M).hom.mapPresheaf).naturality i.op
2157	
2158	/-- **Global sections of a pullback along an open immersion are sections over the range**
2159	(gap1, Hfr section transport). The `U = ⊤` instance of `gammaPullbackImageIso`:
2160	`Γ((pullback f).obj M, ⊤) ≅ Γ(M, f.opensRange)`, using `f ''ᵁ ⊤ = f.opensRange`. Once this lands the
2161	named-form descent `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` and gap1 follow. -/
2162	noncomputable def gammaPullbackTopIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f]
2163	    (M : Y.Modules) :
2164	    Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M, ⊤) ≅ Γ(M, f.opensRange) :=
2165	  gammaPullbackImageIso f M ⊤ ≪≫ eqToIso (by rw [Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange])
2166	
2167	/-- **Open-immersion structure-sheaf ring iso on an image open** (gap1, Hfr semilinearity).
2168	For an open immersion `j : X ⟶ Y` and an open `V ⊆ X`, the immersion is an isomorphism onto its
2169	image `j ''ᵁ V`, so pulling structure-sheaf sections back gives a ring isomorphism
2170	`σ_V : Γ(X, V) ≃+* Γ(Y, j ''ᵁ V)`. This is `(j.appIso V)⁻¹` packaged as a `RingEquiv`; it is the
2171	`σ` along which `gammaPullbackImageIso_hom_semilinear` is semilinear, the form bridge (I)
2172	`isLocalizedModule_of_ringEquiv_semilinear` consumes.
2173	
2174	The direction is source `→` image (so `σ_V a` lands in `Γ(Y, j ''ᵁ V)` for `a : Γ(X, V)`, the
2175	section ring acting on the pullback module's sections). Project-local. -/
2176	noncomputable def gammaImageRingEquiv {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (j : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion j]
2177	    (V : X.Opens) : Γ(X, V) ≃+* Γ(Y, j ''ᵁ V) :=
2178	  (j.appIso V).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv.symm
2179	
2180	/-- **Semilinearity of the pullback section transport** (gap1 semilinearity wall). The forward map
2181	of `gammaPullbackImageIso` is `σ_V`-semilinear (`σ_V = gammaImageRingEquiv`): for `a : Γ(X, V)` a
2182	section of the structure sheaf and `x` a section of the pullback module,
2183	`hom (a • x) = σ_V a • hom x`. The pullback-side action is the structure-sheaf action through
2184	the pullback's `mapPresheaf`; the action on the `M` side is `M`'s action through `σ_V`.
2185	Project-local. -/
2186	theorem gammaPullbackImageIso_hom_semilinear {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (j : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion j]
2187	    (M : Y.Modules) (V : X.Opens) (a : Γ(X, V))
2188	    (x : Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj M, V)) :
2189	    (gammaPullbackImageIso j M V).hom (a • x)
2190	      = gammaImageRingEquiv j V a • (gammaPullbackImageIso j M V).hom x := by
2191	  -- `gammaPullbackImageIso j M V`'s forward map is `Γ(-, V)` of the `Ab`-morphism
2192	  -- `ψ := ((restrictFunctorIsoPullback j).symm.app M).hom`, i.e. the section map `ψ.app V`.
2193	  simp only [gammaPullbackImageIso, Functor.mapIso_hom, Functor.comp_map,
2194	    Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf_map, CategoryTheory.evaluation_obj_map,
2195	    Scheme.Modules.mapPresheaf_app]
2196	  -- `ψ.app V` is `Γ(X, V)`-linear (`Hom.app_smul`): `ψ.app V (a • x) = a • ψ.app V x`, the
2197	  -- `Γ(X, V)`-action being `restrictFunctor`'s `restrictScalars`-action along `(j.appIso V).inv`.
2198	  erw [Scheme.Modules.Hom.app_smul]
2199	  -- The `restrictScalars` action `a •_{restrict} m` is defeq to `(j.appIso V).inv a •_M m`,

You are exploring a Lean 4 project at /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge. Search breadth: very thorough.

The predicate (AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean:1416):
```lean
def CoherentSheafFlat {X S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
  ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) {V : X.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
    letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V) := Module.compHom _ (f.appLE U V e).hom
    Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V)
```

Task: find and report every place in AlgebraicJacobian/ where `CoherentSheafFlat` is PRODUCED (proved) or CONSUMED (hypothesis used), especially in GenericFlatnessGeometric.lean, FlatteningStratificationUniversal.lean, QuotScheme.lean. grep -rn "CoherentSheafFlat" AlgebraicJacobian/. For each site report:
(a) exact declaration name + file:line,
(b) HOW the proof handles the `letI Module.compHom` instance: quote the exact tactic/term incantations used to (i) introduce the compHom module structure, (ii) set up `IsScalarTower` via `of_algebraMap_smul`, (iii) transfer `Module.Flat` between different module instances on the same carrier (e.g. `Module.Flat.of_...`, `change`, `exact` with defeq, `.toAlgebra` tricks, `hact : ... = ...` rewrites of ring map equalities).
(c) Any helper/bridging lemmas with `appLE` in them that relate `f.appLE U V e` to compositions (e.g. `appLE_comp_appLE`) or to `algebraMap` — quote them.

Also: read the GenericFreeness section inside AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean (search for "GenericFreeness" section header) and report the recipe used there for two-localization/base-change flatness/freeness transports: specifically any use of `(IsLocalization.Away.awayToAwayRight f g).toAlgebra`, `IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq`, `TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange`, `Module.Flat.baseChange`, `Module.Flat.trans`, `IsLocalization.flat`, `Localization.flat` — quote the exact patterns (10-30 line excerpts) so they can be imitated.

Also grep for `flatLocus` in AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/ and report how flatLocus proofs bridge CoherentSheafFlat-style compHom flatness to localized flatness (which lemmas they invoke).

Also report the exact signature of `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen` and `restrictBasicOpenₗ` in AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean (grep for their definitions; restrictBasicOpenₗ is a LinearMap — report over which ring, source and target modules, and required instance binders).

Return a structured report with file paths, line numbers, verbatim excerpts. Do NOT write any files.
