670	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — affine pullback dictionary -/
671	
672	/-- **Naturality of the Γ-fragment comparison.** The per-object isomorphisms
673	`gammaPushforwardIso φ N : Γ_R((Spec φ)_* N) ≅ restrictScalars φ (Γ_{R'} N)` assemble into a
674	natural isomorphism of functors `(Spec R').Modules ⥤ ModuleCat R`
675	\[ (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* \;\circ\; \Gamma_R
676	   \;\cong\; \Gamma_{R'} \;\circ\; \operatorname{restr}_\varphi . \]
677	Every constituent of `gammaPushforwardIso` is the identity on underlying elements (the
678	`restrictScalarsComp'App` isos and `restrictScalarsCongr` merely repackage the module structure
679	on the unchanged carrier), so naturality is a pointwise `rfl`. This is the right-adjoint natural
680	isomorphism that drives the affine pullback dictionary `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` via
681	`Adjunction.natIsoOfRightAdjointNatIso`. Project-local. -/
682	noncomputable def gammaPushforwardNatIso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (φ : R ⟶ R') :
683	    Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ) ⋙ moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R) ≅
684	      moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R') ⋙ ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom :=
685	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun N => gammaPushforwardIso φ N) (by
686	    intro N N' g
687	    ext x
688	    rfl)
689	
690	/-- **Affine pullback of a tilde-module.** For a ring map `φ : R ⟶ R'` and an `R`-module `M`,
691	pulling the quasi-coherent sheaf `M^~` back along `Spec φ` is, up to canonical isomorphism, the
692	tilde of the extension of scalars `R' ⊗_R M`. This is the pullback companion of
693	`pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` and is part (1) of Stacks Tag 01I9 (`lemma-widetilde-pullback`).
694	
695	The construction is the uniqueness-of-left-adjoints route. Pullback along `Spec φ` is left adjoint
696	to pushforward, and `tilde` is left adjoint to global sections; their composite
697	`tilde_R ⋙ pullback` is left adjoint to `pushforward ⋙ Γ_R`. Symmetrically `extendScalars φ` is
698	left adjoint to `restrictScalars φ`, so `extendScalars ⋙ tilde_{R'}` is left adjoint to
699	`Γ_{R'} ⋙ restrictScalars φ`. The two right adjoints are identified by the natural isomorphism
700	`gammaPushforwardNatIso` (which is the global-sections comparison `gammaPushforwardIso`), so
701	`Adjunction.natIsoOfRightAdjointNatIso` yields an isomorphism of the two left adjoints; evaluating
702	at `M` gives the claim. Project-local: the affine companion of the pushforward dictionary. See
703	blueprint `lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso`. -/
704	noncomputable def pullback_spec_tilde_iso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}}
705	    (φ : R ⟶ R') (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) :
706	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M) ≅
707	      tilde ((ModuleCat.extendScalars φ.hom).obj M) :=
708	  let adjL := (tilde.adjunction (R := R)).comp
709	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map φ))
710	  let adjR := (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj φ.hom).comp (tilde.adjunction (R := R'))
711	  (((conjugateIsoEquiv adjL adjR).symm (gammaPushforwardNatIso φ)).symm).app M
712	
713	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — affine termwise base-change brick (IN PROGRESS)
714	
715	The concrete-tilde route to flat base change (Stacks 02KH) builds a *fresh* base-change
716	isomorphism `e : g^* ∘ f_* ≅ f'_* ∘ (g')^*` by gluing affine-local isomorphisms, instead of
717	proving the canonical adjoint mate `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is an isomorphism (which walls on the
718	mate ↔ `cancelBaseChange` coherence). The affine-local brick is the floor of that construction:
719	over the fully affine pushout square
720	```
721	  Spec (A ⊗_R R') --Spec ρ--> Spec A
722	  |Spec σ                      |Spec φ
723	  v                            v
724	  Spec R'      ----Spec ψ---->  Spec R
725	```
726	(`φ : R → A`, `ψ : R → R'`, `ρ = includeLeft`, `σ = includeRight` into `A ⊗_R R'`) it produces the
727	termwise isomorphism `g^*(f_* M̃) ≅ f'_*((g')^* M̃)`, assembled from the two sorry-free tilde
728	dictionaries `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` / `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` and the pure
729	commutative-algebra cancellation `Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange`, never forming the adjoint
730	mate.
731	
732	STATUS (iter-305): the load-bearing module-level core — the `R'`-linear iso
733	`(extendScalars ψ) ∘ (restrictScalars φ) ≅ (restrictScalars σ) ∘ (extendScalars ρ)` realising
734	`R' ⊗_R M ≅ (A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M` — was PROVEN to typecheck axiom-clean in the **CommRingCat framing**
735	(ambient algebras supplied as `φ.hom.toAlgebra` / `ψ.hom.toAlgebra`), by `@`-threading the
736	`RingHom.toAlgebra` instances through `Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange` (so its output is defeq to
737	the `compHom`-based `ModuleCat.extendScalars`/`restrictScalars` objects) with `IsPushout` transported
738	from `TensorProduct.isPushout'` via `Algebra.algebra_ext`. See `task_results` for the full working
739	construction. It is NOT yet landed as a top-level declaration: stating the tensor `A ⊗_R R'` in the
740	signature forces a Type-u framing whose *opaque* ambient `[Algebra R A]`/`[Algebra R R']` lets the
741	GLOBAL `Algebra.TensorProduct.leftAlgebra`/`rightAlgebra` instances win over the `toAlgebra` `letI`s,
742	breaking the `compHom`-defeq on the inner `Module A (A⊗_R R')` and outer `Module R' (·)`. The
743	recommended fix (iter-306) is the abstract-`B` framing: take `ρ : A ⟶ B`, `σ : R' ⟶ B` and a
744	`CategoryTheory.IsPushout φ ψ ρ σ` (or `IsPullback` of the Spec square) hypothesis, derive
745	`Algebra.IsPushout` in the proof with all algebras `toAlgebra` (no global tensor instances to shadow),
746	and assemble the 5-step chain. See blueprint `lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange`. -/
747	
748	/-- **Module-level base-change cancellation iso (abstract-`B` framing).** Given a pushout square of
749	commutative rings
750	```
751	  R --φ--> A
752	  |ψ       |ρ
753	  v        v
754	  R' --σ-> B
755	```
756	(so `B ≅ A ⊗_R R'`) and an `A`-module `M`, the `R'`-linear isomorphism
757	`extendScalars_ψ (restrictScalars_φ M) ≅ restrictScalars_σ (extendScalars_ρ M)`, i.e.
758	`R' ⊗_R M ≅ restrictScalars_σ (B ⊗_A M)`. It realises the inverse of Mathlib's
759	`Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange` between the extension-of-scalars objects attached to the two
760	legs of the square. The abstract `B` (rather than the concrete `A ⊗_R R'`) keeps all ambient
761	algebra instances `toAlgebra`, so no global tensor-product instance shadows them and
762	`cancelBaseChange`'s output module structures land defeq on the `compHom`-based
763	`extendScalars`/`restrictScalars` objects. Project-local: the module core of the affine termwise
764	base-change brick. See blueprint `lem:baseChangeCancelModuleIso`. -/
765	noncomputable def baseChangeCancelModuleIso {R A R' B : CommRingCat.{u}}
766	    (φ : R ⟶ A) (ψ : R ⟶ R') (ρ : A ⟶ B) (σ : R' ⟶ B)
767	    (h : CategoryTheory.IsPushout φ ψ ρ σ) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
768	    (ModuleCat.extendScalars ψ.hom).obj ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M) ≅
769	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars σ.hom).obj ((ModuleCat.extendScalars ρ.hom).obj M) := by
770	  letI iRA : Algebra (R : Type u) (A : Type u) := φ.hom.toAlgebra
771	  letI iRR' : Algebra (R : Type u) (R' : Type u) := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
772	  letI iAB : Algebra (A : Type u) (B : Type u) := ρ.hom.toAlgebra
773	  letI iR'B : Algebra (R' : Type u) (B : Type u) := σ.hom.toAlgebra
774	  letI iRB : Algebra (R : Type u) (B : Type u) := (ρ.hom.comp φ.hom).toAlgebra
775	  letI iMR : Module (R : Type u) M := ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M).isModule
776	  haveI tRAM : IsScalarTower (R : Type u) A M :=
777	    ⟨fun r a m => by rw [Algebra.smul_def, mul_smul]; rfl⟩
778	  haveI tRAB : @IsScalarTower (R : Type u) A B _ iAB.toSMul iRB.toSMul :=
779	    @IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq (R : Type u) A B _ _ _ iRA iAB iRB (fun _ => rfl)
780	  haveI tRR'B : @IsScalarTower (R : Type u) R' B _ iR'B.toSMul iRB.toSMul :=
781	    @IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq (R : Type u) R' B _ _ _ iRR' iR'B iRB (fun r => by
782	      change (ρ.hom.comp φ.hom) r = σ.hom (ψ.hom r)
783	      have := congrArg (fun m => (CommRingCat.Hom.hom m) r) h.w
784	      simpa using this)
785	  haveI hpush : @Algebra.IsPushout (R : Type u) R' _ _ iRR' A B _ _ iRA iR'B iAB iRB tRAB tRR'B :=
786	    (CommRingCat.isPushout_iff_isPushout).mp h.flip
787	  exact LinearEquiv.toModuleIso
788	    (@Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange (R : Type u) R' _ _ iRR' A B _ _ iRA iRB iAB iR'B
789	      tRAB tRR'B hpush M _ iMR _ tRAM).symm
790	
791	/-- **Affine termwise base change (abstract-`B` framing).** For a pushout square of commutative
792	rings `(φ : R ⟶ A, ψ : R ⟶ R', ρ : A ⟶ B, σ : R' ⟶ B)` — equivalently the cartesian square of
793	affine schemes obtained by `Spec` — and an `A`-module `M`, the base-change comparison
794	`g^*(f_* M̃) ≅ f'_*((g')^* M̃)` for `f = Spec φ`, `g = Spec ψ`, `f' = Spec σ`, `g' = Spec ρ`. It is
795	assembled as a 5-step chain from the two affine tilde dictionaries
796	(`pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` / `pullback_spec_tilde_iso`) and the module-level cancellation core
797	`baseChangeCancelModuleIso`, and it **never** forms the adjoint mate
798	`pushforwardBaseChangeMap`, so it sidesteps the unresolved mate ↔ `cancelBaseChange` coherence
799	obligation of the canonical route. This is the affine-local brick (the floor) of the active
800	concrete-tilde route to flat base change (Stacks 02KG/02KH). Project-local. See blueprint
801	`lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange`. -/
802	noncomputable def affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange {R A R' B : CommRingCat.{u}}
803	    (φ : R ⟶ A) (ψ : R ⟶ R') (ρ : A ⟶ B) (σ : R' ⟶ B)
804	    (h : CategoryTheory.IsPushout φ ψ ρ σ) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
805	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map ψ)).obj
806	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M)) ≅
807	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map σ)).obj
808	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map ρ)).obj (tilde M)) :=
809	  (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map ψ)).mapIso (pushforward_spec_tilde_iso φ M)
810	    ≪≫ pullback_spec_tilde_iso ψ ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M)
811	    ≪≫ (tilde.functor R').mapIso (baseChangeCancelModuleIso φ ψ ρ σ h M)
812	    ≪≫ (pushforward_spec_tilde_iso σ ((ModuleCat.extendScalars ρ.hom).obj M)).symm
813	    ≪≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map σ)).mapIso (pullback_spec_tilde_iso ρ M).symm
814	
815	
816	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — cancellation/localization compatibility
817	
818	For a tower of commutative rings `R → S → S'` and a further homomorphism `R → A`, the
819	cancellation isomorphism `Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange` for the `S'`-tower is the
820	base change along `S → S'` of the cancellation isomorphism for the `S`-tower. This is the
821	purely commutative-algebraic "cancellation move" of the affine-restriction naturality square
822	(`pushforwardPullbackBaseChange_restrict_naturality`); see blueprint
823	`lem:cancelBaseChange_localization_compat`. It carries no scheme scaffolding.
824	-/
825	
826	section CancelBaseChangeCompat

442	`cech_degree_affine_baseChange`.  Project-local; blueprint `lem:pushPullObj_iso_tilde` (altitude 2). -/
443	noncomputable def pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
444	    (f : X ⟶ Spec R) (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent)
445	    {V : X.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) :
446	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.ι)) ≅
447	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map (Spec.preimage (hV.fromSpec ≫ f)))).obj

462	for the canonical affine identification.  The pushed-forward push–pull object
463	`(pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.ι))` is canonically isomorphic to the altitude-2
464	`(Spec φ)_*(tilde N)` form, **transported back along `e_S⁻¹`** so it lands in `O_S`-modules rather
465	than `O_{Spec Γ(S)}`-modules, where `φ := Spec.preimage (hV.fromSpec ≫ (f ≫ e_S.hom))` presents
466	`(e_S ∘ f) ∘ (isoSpec ∘ j_V)` as `Spec φ`.

469	so the literal-Spec bridge `pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde` applies to it; then conjugate by
470	`(pushforward e_S.inv)` and collapse `pushforward e_S.hom ⋙ pushforward e_S.inv ≅ id` via

475	    (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent)

477	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.ι)) ≅
478	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward S.isoSpec.inv).obj
479	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map (Spec.preimage
480	            (hV.fromSpec ≫ (f ≫ S.isoSpec.hom))))).obj
481	          (tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj

484	  -- `collapse : (e_S⁻¹)_* ((f ≫ e_S)_* P) ≅ f_* P`, the `e_S` cancellation; take `.symm` to start
485	  -- from `f_* P`, then push the literal bridge through `(e_S⁻¹)_*`.
486	  ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward S.isoSpec.inv).mapIso
487	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp f S.isoSpec.hom).symm.app
488	          (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.ι))) ≪≫
489	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp S.isoSpec.hom S.isoSpec.inv).app
490	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.ι))) ≪≫
491	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr S.isoSpec.hom_inv_id).app
492	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.ι))) ≪≫
493	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId S).app
494	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.ι)))).symm ≪≫

498	/-- **Čech intersection opens are affine** (separated case).  For a separated `f : X ⟶ S`

500	intersection open `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ = ⨅ k, (𝒰.f (σ k)).opensRange` is affine.  `X` is
501	separated over the terminal scheme (`f` separated and `S` affine — hence separated — so the
502	composite `terminal.from X = f ≫ terminal.from S` is separated), so the absolute diagonal of
503	`X` is a closed immersion, hence affine; finite intersections of affine opens of a scheme with
504	affine diagonal are affine (`IsAffineOpen.iInf`), and each member open is affine as the range of
505	an open immersion out of the affine `𝒰.X (σ k)` (`isAffineOpen_opensRange`).  This is the

511	  -- `X` is separated over the terminal scheme: `terminal.from X = f ≫ terminal.from S`, with
512	  -- `f` separated and `terminal.from S` separated (`S` affine ⟹ `S.IsSeparated`).

516	  -- hence the absolute diagonal is a closed immersion (⟹ affine), unlocking `IsAffineOpen.iInf`.
517	  haveI : IsClosedImmersion (pullback.diagonal (terminal.from X)) :=
518	    IsSeparated.isClosedImmersion_diagonal
519	  exact IsAffineOpen.iInf (fun k => isAffineOpen_opensRange (𝒰.f (σ k)))
520	
521	/-- **Restriction of the cartesian square over an affine intersection open is a (ring) pushout**
522	(Stacks 02KG; carved block `lem:restricted_cartesian_affine_pushout`).  Restricting the global
523	cartesian square `X' = X ×_S S' → X` over the Čech fibre-power intersection open
524	`V = coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ↪ X` (open immersion `j_σ`) replaces `X` by `V` and `X'` by the fibre
525	product `X' ×_X V`, and the restricted square
526	```

528	   |pullback.snd             |j_σ
529	   v                         v
530	  X'  --------g'------------> X

533	`[IsAffine S]`, `[IsAffine S']` and an affine cover, `V` is affine (`coverInterOpen_isAffine`)
534	and `X' ×_X V` is affine, so applying global sections turns this cartesian square of affines into
535	the cocartesian (pushout) square of rings `R → A_σ`, `R → R'`, `A_σ → A_σ ⊗_R R'` via the

543	    IsPullback (pullback.snd g' (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)))

558	    (f : X ⟶ Spec R) [IsSeparated f]

576	`B`-module restricted to `R'` (resp. read as the corner `B`-module `B ⊗_{A_σ} N`), the tensor base
577	change `N ⊗_R R' = R' ⊗_R N`.  This is precisely the module-level corner identification realised by
578	the inverse of `baseChangeCancelModuleIso`: `restrict_σ'(B ⊗_{A_σ} N) ≅ R' ⊗_R N`.  The geometric
579	wrapping (relating the geometric `Γ(V', (j'_σ)^*((g')^*F))` to `B ⊗_{A_σ} N` via the cartesian

142	    overOpensFunctorUnitIso (X := Y) W

144	    P6.map eV.functor ηV.symm

150	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
151	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 1000000 in
152	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
153	/-- **Pullback preserves quasi-coherence** (Stacks 01BG, general-morphism case).  For an
154	arbitrary morphism of schemes `g : Y ⟶ X` and a quasi-coherent `F : X.Modules`, the module
155	pullback `g^* F` is quasi-coherent.
156	
157	Quasi-coherence is local (`SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop`): the preimages
158	under `g` of the quasi-coherence cover of `X` cover `Y`, and over each preimage the
159	per-slice obligation is `presentationPullbackSliceOfOver`. -/
160	theorem pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom {Y X : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
161	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) :
162	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).IsQuasicoherent := by
163	  obtain ⟨⟨q⟩⟩ := hF
164	  -- The preimages of the quasi-coherence cover of `X` cover `Y`.
165	  have hcov : (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).CoversTop
166	      (fun i => g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i) := by
167	    intro W x hx
168	    obtain ⟨U', fU, hf, hU'⟩ := q.coversTop ⊤ (g.base x) (by trivial)
169	    obtain ⟨i, ⟨gi⟩⟩ := hf
170	    refine ⟨W ⊓ (g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i), homOfLE inf_le_left, ⟨i, ⟨homOfLE inf_le_right⟩⟩,
171	      ⟨hx, ?_⟩⟩
172	    exact (leOfHom gi) hU'
173	  -- Per-slice presentations through the restricted-pullback bridge.
174	  haveI hslice : ∀ i,
175	      (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i)).IsQuasicoherent := fun i =>
176	    (presentationPullbackSliceOfOver g F (q.X i) (q.presentation i)).isQuasicoherent
177	  exact SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop
178	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F) (fun i => g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i) hcov
179	
180	end AlgebraicGeometry

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2024 Junyan Xu. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Junyan Xu
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Flat.Stability
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalProperties.Exactness
10	
11	/-!
12	# Flatness and localization
13	
14	In this file we show that localizations are flat, and flatness is a local property.
15	
16	## Main result
17	* `IsLocalization.flat`: a localization of a commutative ring is flat over it.
18	* `Module.flat_iff_of_isLocalization` : Let `Rₚ` a localization of a commutative ring `R`
19	  and `M` be a module over `Rₚ`. Then `M` is flat over `R` if and only if `M` is flat over `Rₚ`.
20	* `Module.flat_of_isLocalized_maximal` : Let `M` be a module over a commutative ring `R`.
21	  If the localization of `M` at each maximal ideal `P` is flat over `Rₚ`, then `M` is flat over `R`.
22	* `Module.flat_of_isLocalized_span` : Let `M` be a module over a commutative ring `R`
23	  and `S` be a set that spans `R`. If the localization of `M` at each `s : S` is flat
24	  over `Localization.Away s`, then `M` is flat over `R`.
25	-/
26	
27	public section
28	
29	open IsLocalizedModule LocalizedModule LinearMap TensorProduct
30	
31	variable {R : Type*} (S : Type*) [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring S] [Algebra R S]
32	variable (p : Submonoid R) [IsLocalization p S]
33	variable (M : Type*) [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M] [Module S M] [IsScalarTower R S M]
34	
35	include p in
36	theorem IsLocalization.flat : Module.Flat R S := by
37	  refine Module.Flat.iff_lTensor_injectiveₛ.mpr fun P _ _ N ↦ ?_
38	  have h := ((range N.subtype).isLocalizedModule S p (TensorProduct.mk R S P 1)).isBaseChange _ S
39	  let e := (LinearEquiv.ofInjective _ Subtype.val_injective).lTensor S ≪≫ₗ h.equiv.restrictScalars R
40	  have : N.subtype.lTensor S = Submodule.subtype _ ∘ₗ e.toLinearMap := by
41	    ext; change _ = (h.equiv _).1; simp [h.equiv_tmul, TensorProduct.smul_tmul']
42	  simpa [this] using! e.injective
43	
44	instance Localization.flat [Module.Flat R S] (p : Submonoid S) : Module.Flat R (Localization p) :=
45	  have : Module.Flat S (Localization p) := IsLocalization.flat _ p
46	  .trans R S _
47	
48	namespace Module
49	
50	include p in
51	theorem flat_iff_of_isLocalization : Flat S M ↔ Flat R M :=
52	  have := isLocalizedModule_id p M S
53	  have := IsLocalization.flat S p
54	  ⟨fun _ ↦ .trans R S M, fun _ ↦ .of_isLocalizedModule S p .id⟩
55	
56	variable (Mₚ : ∀ (P : Ideal S) [P.IsMaximal], Type*)
57	  [∀ (P : Ideal S) [P.IsMaximal], AddCommMonoid (Mₚ P)]
58	  [∀ (P : Ideal S) [P.IsMaximal], Module R (Mₚ P)]
59	  [∀ (P : Ideal S) [P.IsMaximal], Module S (Mₚ P)]
60	  [∀ (P : Ideal S) [P.IsMaximal], IsScalarTower R S (Mₚ P)]
61	  (f : ∀ (P : Ideal S) [P.IsMaximal], M →ₗ[S] Mₚ P)
62	  [∀ (P : Ideal S) [P.IsMaximal], IsLocalizedModule.AtPrime P (f P)]
63	
64	include f in
65	theorem flat_of_isLocalized_maximal (H : ∀ (P : Ideal S) [P.IsMaximal], Flat R (Mₚ P)) :
66	    Module.Flat R M := by
67	  simp_rw [Flat.iff_lTensor_injectiveₛ] at H ⊢
68	  simp_rw [← AlgebraTensorModule.coe_lTensor (A := S)]
69	  refine fun _ _ _ N ↦ injective_of_isLocalized_maximal _
70	    (fun P ↦ AlgebraTensorModule.rTensor R _ (f P)) _
71	    (fun P ↦ AlgebraTensorModule.rTensor R _ (f P)) _ fun P hP ↦ ?_
72	  simpa [IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor] using H P N
73	
74	theorem flat_of_localized_maximal
75	    (h : ∀ (P : Ideal R) [P.IsMaximal], Flat R (LocalizedModule P.primeCompl M)) :
76	    Flat R M :=
77	  flat_of_isLocalized_maximal _ _ _ (fun _ _ ↦ mkLinearMap _ _) h
78	
79	variable (s : Set S) (spn : Ideal.span s = ⊤)
80	  (Mₛ : ∀ _ : s, Type*)
81	  [∀ r : s, AddCommMonoid (Mₛ r)]
82	  [∀ r : s, Module R (Mₛ r)]
83	  [∀ r : s, Module S (Mₛ r)]
84	  [∀ r : s, IsScalarTower R S (Mₛ r)]
85	  (g : ∀ r : s, M →ₗ[S] Mₛ r)
86	  [∀ r : s, IsLocalizedModule.Away r.1 (g r)]
87	include spn
88	
89	include g in
90	theorem flat_of_isLocalized_span (H : ∀ r : s, Module.Flat R (Mₛ r)) :
91	    Module.Flat R M := by
92	  simp_rw [Flat.iff_lTensor_injectiveₛ] at H ⊢
93	  simp_rw [← AlgebraTensorModule.coe_lTensor (A := S)]
94	  refine fun _ _ _ N ↦ injective_of_isLocalized_span s spn _
95	    (fun r ↦ AlgebraTensorModule.rTensor R _ (g r)) _
96	    (fun r ↦ AlgebraTensorModule.rTensor R _ (g r)) _ fun r ↦ ?_
97	  simpa [IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor] using H r N
98	
99	theorem flat_of_localized_span
100	    (h : ∀ r : s, Flat S (LocalizedModule.Away r.1 M)) :
101	    Flat S M :=
102	  flat_of_isLocalized_span _ _ _ spn _ (fun _ ↦ mkLinearMap _ _) h
103	
104	end Module
105	
106	variable {A B : Type*} [CommRing A] [CommRing B] [Algebra A B]
107	
108	instance [Module.Flat A B] (p : Ideal A) [p.IsPrime] (P : Ideal B) [P.IsPrime] [P.LiesOver p]
109	    [Algebra (Localization.AtPrime p) (Localization.AtPrime P)]
110	    [Localization.AtPrime.IsLiesOverAlgebra p P] :
111	    Module.Flat (Localization.AtPrime p) (Localization.AtPrime P) := by
112	  rw [Module.flat_iff_of_isLocalization (Localization.AtPrime p) p.primeCompl]
113	  exact Module.Flat.trans A B (Localization.AtPrime P)
114	
115	section IsSMulRegular
116	
117	variable {M} in
118	theorem IsSMulRegular.of_isLocalizedModule {K : Type*} [AddCommMonoid K] [Module R K]
119	    (f : K →ₗ[R] M) [IsLocalizedModule p f] {x : R} (reg : IsSMulRegular K x) :
120	    IsSMulRegular M (algebraMap R S x) :=
121	  have : Module.Flat R S := IsLocalization.flat S p
122	  reg.of_flat_of_isBaseChange (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange p S f)
123	
124	include p in
125	theorem IsSMulRegular.of_isLocalization {x : R} (reg : IsSMulRegular R x) :
126	    IsSMulRegular S (algebraMap R S x) :=
127	  reg.of_isLocalizedModule S p (Algebra.linearMap R S)
128	
129	end IsSMulRegular
130

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2024 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Flat.Basic
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.IsTensorProduct
10	public import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Tower
11	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.BaseChange
12	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LocalizedModule.Basic
13	
14	/-!
15	# Flatness is stable under composition and base change
16	
17	We show that flatness is stable under composition and base change.
18	
19	## Main theorems
20	
21	* `Module.Flat.trans`: if `S` is a flat `R`-algebra and `M` is a flat `S`-module,
22	                      then `M` is a flat `R`-module
23	* `Module.Flat.baseChange`: if `M` is a flat `R`-module and `S` is any `R`-algebra,
24	                            then `S ⊗[R] M` is `S`-flat.
25	* `Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule`: if `M` is a flat `R`-module and `S` is a submonoid of `R`
26	                                          then the localization of `M` at `S` is flat as a module
27	                                          for the localization of `R` at `S`.
28	-/
29	
30	public section
31	
32	universe u v w t t'
33	
34	open Function (Injective Surjective)
35	
36	open LinearMap (lsmul rTensor lTensor)
37	
38	open TensorProduct
39	
40	namespace Module.Flat
41	
42	section Composition
43	
44	/-! ### Composition
45	
46	Let `R` be a ring, `S` a flat `R`-algebra and `M` a flat `S`-module. To show that `M` is flat
47	as an `R`-module, we show that the inclusion of an `R`-submodule `N` into an `R`-module `P`
48	tensored on the left with `M` is injective. For this consider the composition of natural maps
49	
50	`M ⊗[R] N ≃ M ⊗[S] (S ⊗[R] N) → M ⊗[S] (S ⊗[R] P) ≃ M ⊗[R] P`;
51	
52	`S ⊗[R] N → S ⊗[R] P` is injective by `R`-flatness of `S`,
53	so the middle map is injective by `S`-flatness of `M`.
54	-/
55	
56	variable (R : Type u) (S : Type v) (M : Type w)
57	  [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring S] [Algebra R S]
58	  [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M] [Module S M] [IsScalarTower R S M]
59	
60	open AlgebraTensorModule in
61	/-- If `S` is a flat `R`-algebra, then any flat `S`-Module is also `R`-flat. -/
62	theorem trans [Flat R S] [Flat S M] : Flat R M := by
63	  rw [Flat.iff_lTensor_injectiveₛ]
64	  introv
65	  rw [← coe_lTensor (A := S), ← EquivLike.injective_comp (cancelBaseChange R S S _ _),
66	    ← LinearEquiv.coe_coe, ← LinearMap.coe_comp, lTensor_comp_cancelBaseChange,
67	    LinearMap.coe_comp, LinearEquiv.coe_coe, EquivLike.comp_injective]
68	  iterate 2 apply Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap
69	  exact Subtype.val_injective
70	
71	variable {R M} in
72	@[simp]
73	lemma ulift_left_iff : Flat (ULift.{t} R) M ↔ Flat R M := by
74	  refine ⟨fun h ↦ .trans _ (ULift R) _, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
75	  have : Module.Flat (ULift.{t} R) R := .of_ulift
76	  let _ := ULift.algebra'
77	  exact .trans _ R _
78	
79	variable {R M} in
80	@[simp]
81	lemma ulift_right_iff : Flat R (ULift.{t} M) ↔ Flat R M :=
82	  Flat.equiv_iff ULift.moduleEquiv
83	
84	end Composition
85	
86	section BaseChange
87	
88	/-! ### Base change
89	
90	Let `R` be a ring, `M` a flat `R`-module and `S` an `R`-algebra, then
91	`S ⊗[R] M` is a flat `S`-module. This is a special case of `Module.Flat.instTensorProduct`.
92	
93	-/
94	
95	variable (R : Type u) (S : Type v) (M : Type w)
96	  [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring S] [Algebra R S]
97	  [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M]
98	
99	/-- If `M` is a flat `R`-module and `S` is any `R`-algebra, `S ⊗[R] M` is `S`-flat. -/
100	instance baseChange [Flat R M] : Flat S (S ⊗[R] M) := inferInstance
101	
102	/-- A base change of a flat module is flat. -/
103	theorem isBaseChange [Flat R M] (N : Type t) [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R N] [Module S N]
104	    [IsScalarTower R S N] {f : M →ₗ[R] N} (h : IsBaseChange S f) :
105	    Flat S N :=
106	  of_linearEquiv (IsBaseChange.equiv h).symm
107	
108	end BaseChange
109	
110	section Localization
111	
112	variable {R : Type u} {M Mp : Type*} (Rp : Type v)
113	  [CommSemiring R] [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M] [CommSemiring Rp] [Algebra R Rp]
114	  [AddCommMonoid Mp] [Module R Mp] [Module Rp Mp] [IsScalarTower R Rp Mp]
115	
116	instance localizedModule [Flat R M] (S : Submonoid R) :
117	    Flat (Localization S) (LocalizedModule S M) := by
118	  apply Flat.isBaseChange (R := R) (S := Localization S)
119	    (f := LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap S M)
120	  rw [← isLocalizedModule_iff_isBaseChange S]
121	  exact localizedModuleIsLocalizedModule S
122	
123	theorem of_isLocalizedModule [Flat R M] (S : Submonoid R) [IsLocalization S Rp]
124	    (f : M →ₗ[R] Mp) [h : IsLocalizedModule S f] : Flat Rp Mp := by
125	  fapply Flat.isBaseChange (R := R) (M := M) (S := Rp) (N := Mp)
126	  exact (isLocalizedModule_iff_isBaseChange S Rp f).mp h
127	
128	instance {A : Type*} [CommSemiring A] [Algebra R A] [Flat R A] (S : Submonoid R) :
129	    Flat (Localization S) (Localization (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S)) :=
130	  of_isLocalizedModule _ S (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom R A _).toLinearMap
131	
132	end Localization
133	
134	end Module.Flat
135

1381	namespace Scheme
1382	
1383	/-! ## §1. Coherent-sheaf flatness over a base
1384	
1385	For a morphism `f : X ⟶ S` of schemes and a coherent (more generally:
1386	quasi-coherent) `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, the *coherent-sheaf flatness* predicate
1387	`CoherentSheafFlat f 𝓕` captures Stacks 00HB lifted to the sheaf level:
1388	for every point `x ∈ X` the stalk `𝓕_x` is a flat `𝓞_{S, f(x)}`-module
1389	via the composite `𝓞_{S, f(x)} → 𝓞_{X, x} → End(𝓕_x)`.
1390	
1391	Affine-locally (Stacks 00HB equivalence): on an affine open `U = Spec A
1392	⊆ S` and an affine open `V = Spec B ⊆ f⁻¹U` with `𝓕|_V = M̃` for a
1393	finite `B`-module `M`, the condition becomes `Module.Flat A M` with the
1394	`A`-module structure inherited via the ring map `A → B`. The
1395	file-internal encoding uses this affine-local form.
1396	
1397	Blueprint reference: `def:coherent_sheaf_flat`. -/
1398	
1399	/-- A coherent sheaf `𝓕` on `X` is **flat over `S`** for a morphism
1400	`f : X ⟶ S` if, for every affine open `U ⊆ S` and every affine open
1401	`V ⊆ f⁻¹U`, the `𝓞_X`-module of sections `Γ(𝓕, V)` is flat as a
1402	`Γ(S, U)`-module, where the `Γ(S, U)`-action is the restriction of
1403	scalars along the ring morphism `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V)` (the `appLE`
1404	morphism of `f`).
1405	
1406	This is the affine-local form of Stacks 00HB (a coherent sheaf is flat
1407	over the base if every stalk is flat over the corresponding base
1408	stalk); the affine-local form and stalk form are equivalent for
1409	*quasi-coherent* sheaves on a noetherian base, which is the regime of
1410	the flattening-stratification theorem.
1411	
1412	iter-177+: the body of subsequent flat-locus theorems may package this
1413	predicate as an instance-driven typeclass `Module.Flat`-style if the
1414	sheafified-tensor infrastructure lands in Mathlib; for the iter-176
1415	file-skeleton it is encoded as a `Prop` quantifier. -/
1416	def CoherentSheafFlat {X S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
1417	  ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) {V : X.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
1418	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
1419	    letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V) := Module.compHom _ (f.appLE U V e).hom
1420	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V)
1421	
1422	end Scheme
1423	
1424	/-! ## §2. Generic flatness (Nitsure §4) — see `GenericFlatnessGeometric.lean`
1425	
1426	The geometric generic-flatness theorem `AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness`
1427	(blueprint `thm:generic_flatness`) is stated and **proved** in the separate
1428	file `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GenericFlatnessGeometric`, as the geometric
1429	glue over the algebraic engine of this file: it consumes the qcqs
1430	section-localization engine of `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme`
1431	(Stacks 01P0/01PC/01I8), which this file deliberately does not import so
1432	that the instance environment of the dévissage proofs above stays fixed. -/
1433	
1434	/-! ## §3–§6. Existence, universality, curve specialisation — moved
1435	
1436	The geometric sections (Nitsure §4 sub-lemmas `flatLocusStratification` and
1437	`flatLocusReduction`, the assembly `flatLocusAssembly`, the main theorem
1438	`flatteningStratification`, its universal property, and the curve
1439	specialisation `flatteningStratification.ofCurve`) live in

240	`Module.Finite.trans` (a finite module over a module-finite algebra is finite
241	over the base). This is generic flatness for a *finite* morphism `X → S`. -/
242	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_moduleFinite
243	    (A : Type*) (B : Type*) (M : Type*)
244	    [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
245	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B] [Module.Finite A B]
246	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module.Finite B M]
247	    [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M] :
248	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
249	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
250	  haveI : Module.Finite A M := Module.Finite.trans B M
251	  exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A M
252	
253	/-! ### The dévissage frame for full algebraic generic freeness
254	
255	Grothendieck's generic-freeness lemma (blueprint
256	`thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`, Nitsure §4 "Lemma on Generic Flatness"):
257	for `A` a noetherian domain, `B` a finite-type `A`-algebra and `M` a finite
258	`B`-module there is `f ≠ 0` in `A` with `M_f` free over `A_f`.
259	
260	Nitsure's proof has two layers: a *dévissage* (prime filtration of `M` as a
261	module over the noetherian ring `B`, reducing to `M = B/𝔭`, via the splicing
262	fact that generic freeness is stable under extensions), and a *domain core*
263	(`M = B/𝔭`: Noether normalisation over `Frac A` + clearing denominators +
264	the generic-rank exact sequence + induction on fibre dimension). This
265	section proves both layers completely — the splicing lemma
266	`genericallyFree_of_exact` (via `ModuleCat.free_shortExact` and the
267	localization-transport lemma `free_localizedModule_powers_mul`), the torsion
268	base case `genericallyFree_of_torsion` (Nitsure's `n = -1`), the domain core
269	`genericallyFree_quotient_prime` (Noether normalisation +
270	`genericallyFree_quotient_prime_of_fibre_dim_le`), and the assembly
271	`genericFlatnessAlgebraic` via
272	`IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime`. -/
273	
274	/-- The **generic-freeness property** of an `A`-module `M`: some localization
275	`M_f` at a single non-zero `f ∈ A` is a free module over `A_f`. This is the
276	conclusion shared by every statement of this section. -/
277	def GenericallyFree (A : Type*) (M : Type*) [CommRing A] [AddCommGroup M]
278	    [Module A M] : Prop :=
279	  ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
280	    Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M)
281	
282	/-- Generic freeness transports along enlarging the inverted element: if `M_f`
283	is free over `A_f` then `M_{fg}` is free over `A_{fg}` for every `g`. The
284	proof is base-change associativity
285	`A_{fg} ⊗_{A_f} (A_f ⊗_A M) ≅ A_{fg} ⊗_A M` together with freeness of base
286	change (`Module.Free.tensor`); the two tensor legs are identified with the
287	localized modules by `IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange`. -/
288	theorem free_localizedModule_powers_mul (A : Type*) (M : Type*) [CommRing A]
289	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] (f g : A)
290	    (hf : Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
291	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M)) :
292	    Module.Free (Localization.Away (f * g))
293	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f * g)) M) := by
294	  haveI := hf
295	  letI : Algebra (Localization.Away f) (Localization.Away (f * g)) :=
296	    (IsLocalization.Away.awayToAwayRight (S := Localization.Away f)
297	      (P := Localization.Away (f * g)) f g).toAlgebra
298	  haveI : IsScalarTower A (Localization.Away f) (Localization.Away (f * g)) :=
299	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun a =>
300	      (IsLocalization.Away.awayToAwayRight_eq (S := Localization.Away f)
301	        (P := Localization.Away (f * g)) f g a).symm
302	  have e_f : (Localization.Away f) ⊗[A] M ≃ₗ[Localization.Away f]
303	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M :=
304	    (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers f) (Localization.Away f)
305	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f) M)).equiv
306	  have e_fg : (Localization.Away (f * g)) ⊗[A] M ≃ₗ[Localization.Away (f * g)]
307	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f * g)) M :=
308	    (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers (f * g))
309	      (Localization.Away (f * g))
310	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f * g)) M)).equiv
311	  have e_cancel : (Localization.Away (f * g)) ⊗[Localization.Away f]
312	      ((Localization.Away f) ⊗[A] M) ≃ₗ[Localization.Away (f * g)]
313	      (Localization.Away (f * g)) ⊗[A] M :=
314	    TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange A (Localization.Away f)
315	      (Localization.Away (f * g)) (Localization.Away (f * g)) M
316	  have e_congr : (Localization.Away (f * g)) ⊗[Localization.Away f]
317	      ((Localization.Away f) ⊗[A] M) ≃ₗ[Localization.Away (f * g)]
318	      (Localization.Away (f * g)) ⊗[Localization.Away f]
319	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) :=
320	    TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.congr
321	      (LinearEquiv.refl (Localization.Away (f * g)) (Localization.Away (f * g))) e_f
322	  exact Module.Free.of_equiv'
323	    (inferInstance : Module.Free (Localization.Away (f * g))
324	      ((Localization.Away (f * g)) ⊗[Localization.Away f]
325	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M)))
326	    (e_congr.symm.trans (e_cancel.trans e_fg))
327	
328	/-- Generic freeness is invariant under `A`-linear equivalence. -/
329	theorem GenericallyFree.of_linearEquiv {A : Type*} [CommRing A] {M N : Type*}
330	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module A N]
331	    (e : M ≃ₗ[A] N) (h : GenericallyFree A M) : GenericallyFree A N := by
332	  obtain ⟨f, hf, hfree⟩ := h
333	  refine ⟨f, hf, ?_⟩
334	  haveI := hfree
335	  have e' : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M ≃ₗ[A]
336	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) N :=
337	    IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers f)
338	      ((LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f) N) ∘ₗ e.toLinearMap)
339	  exact Module.Free.of_equiv' hfree
340	    (e'.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (Submonoid.powers f) (Localization.Away f))
341	
342	/-- **Splicing lemma for generic freeness** — the extension step of Nitsure's
343	dévissage. If the outer terms of a short exact sequence
344	`0 → M₁ → M₂ → M₃ → 0` of `A`-modules are generically free, so is the middle
345	term: localize the sequence at the product of the two witnesses (exactness of
346	localization) and apply `ModuleCat.free_shortExact`. -/
347	theorem genericallyFree_of_exact {A : Type*} [CommRing A] [IsDomain A]
348	    {M₁ M₂ M₃ : Type v} [AddCommGroup M₁] [Module A M₁] [AddCommGroup M₂]
349	    [Module A M₂] [AddCommGroup M₃] [Module A M₃]
350	    (u : M₁ →ₗ[A] M₂) (v : M₂ →ₗ[A] M₃) (hu : Function.Injective u)
351	    (hv : Function.Surjective v) (huv : Function.Exact u v)
352	    (h₁ : GenericallyFree A M₁) (h₃ : GenericallyFree A M₃) :
353	    GenericallyFree A M₂ := by
354	  obtain ⟨f₁, hf₁, hfree₁⟩ := h₁
355	  obtain ⟨f₃, hf₃, hfree₃⟩ := h₃
356	  refine ⟨f₁ * f₃, mul_ne_zero hf₁ hf₃, ?_⟩
357	  have hfree₁' := free_localizedModule_powers_mul A M₁ f₁ f₃ hfree₁
358	  have hfree₃' := free_localizedModule_powers_mul A M₃ f₃ f₁ hfree₃
359	  rw [mul_comm f₃ f₁] at hfree₃'
360	  let u' : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₁
361	      →ₗ[Localization.Away (f₁ * f₃)]
362	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₂ :=
363	    (IsLocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃))
364	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₁)
365	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₂)
366	      u).extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃))
367	        (Localization.Away (f₁ * f₃))
368	  let v' : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₂
369	      →ₗ[Localization.Away (f₁ * f₃)]

text: Now let me read the appLE helper lemmas and `Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule_algebra` in GenericFlatnessGeometric.lean, which are central to the compHom handling.

data.content:
80	  `W` and in a chart and lie above base basic opens common to `U` and `U₀`
81	  (`exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter`, twice), and
82	  `Module.flat_of_isLocalized_span` plus two `Module.flat_iff_of_isLocalization`
83	  exchanges assemble the pieces. -/
84	
85	section GenericFlatnessGeometricGlue
86	
87	/-- Elementwise fibre-side `appLE` coherence: restricting the image of `appLE`
88	equals `appLE` into the smaller open. -/
89	private lemma appLE_res_apply {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S) {U : S.Opens}
90	    {W W' : X.Opens} (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U) (h : W' ≤ W) (u : Γ(S, U)) :
91	    (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op).hom ((p.appLE U W e).hom u) =
92	      (p.appLE U W' (h.trans e)).hom u := by
93	  have h1 := congrArg (fun (φ : Γ(S, U) ⟶ Γ(X, W')) => φ.hom u)
94	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE_map (f := p) e (homOfLE h).op)
95	  simpa only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_apply] using h1
96	
97	/-- Elementwise base-side `appLE` coherence: `appLE` of a restricted base section
98	equals `appLE` from the larger base open. -/
99	private lemma appLE_base_res_apply {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S) {U U' : S.Opens}
100	    {W : X.Opens} (h : U' ≤ U) (e' : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U') (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U) (u : Γ(S, U)) :
101	    (p.appLE U' W e').hom ((S.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op).hom u) =
102	      (p.appLE U W e).hom u := by
103	  have h1 := congrArg (fun (φ : Γ(S, U) ⟶ Γ(X, W)) => φ.hom u)
104	    (Scheme.Hom.map_appLE (f := p) e' (homOfLE h).op)
105	  simpa only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_apply] using h1
106	
107	/-- Transport of section flatness along an equality of opens: the `Module.compHom`
108	structures via `appLE` agree definitionally because the `≤`-witness of `appLE` is
109	proof-irrelevant. -/
110	private theorem flat_sections_congr {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules)
111	    {U₀ : S.Opens} {O₁ O₂ : X.Opens} (hEq : O₁ = O₂)
112	    (e₁ : O₁ ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀) (e₂ : O₂ ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀)
113	    (h : letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O₁) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ O₁ e₁).hom
114	         Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O₁)) :
115	    letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O₂) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ O₂ e₂).hom
116	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O₂) := by
117	  subst hEq
118	  exact h
119	
120	open TensorProduct in
121	/-- **Mixed-base stability of flatness under module localization.**  Let `B` be an
122	`R`-algebra, `N` a `B`-module that is flat over `R`, and `g : N →ₗ[B] N'` a
123	localization of `N` at a submonoid `p ⊆ B`.  Then `N'` is still flat over `R`.
124	
125	Mathlib's `Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule` treats only submonoids of `R`
126	itself; here the localization happens on the fibre side.  The proof rewrites
127	`𝟙 N' ⊗ f` as the `IsLocalizedModule.map` of `𝟙 N ⊗ f` (Mathlib's
128	`IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor`, with the `IsLocalizedModule.rTensor` instances)
129	and uses that localization preserves injectivity
130	(`IsLocalizedModule.map_injective`). -/
131	theorem _root_.Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule_algebra {R B : Type v} [CommRing R]
132	    [CommRing B] [Algebra R B] (p : Submonoid B) {N N' : Type v} [AddCommGroup N]
133	    [Module R N] [Module B N] [IsScalarTower R B N] [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N']
134	    [Module B N'] [IsScalarTower R B N'] (g : N →ₗ[B] N') [IsLocalizedModule p g]
135	    [Module.Flat R N] : Module.Flat R N' := by
136	  rw [Module.Flat.iff_lTensor_injectiveₛ]
137	  intro P _ _ I
138	  rw [← TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.coe_lTensor (A := B)]
139	  rw [← IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor (S := p) (g := g) (f := I.subtype)]
140	  exact IsLocalizedModule.map_injective p
141	    (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.rTensor R I g)
142	    (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.rTensor R P g)
143	    (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.lTensor B N I.subtype)
144	    (by
145	      rw [TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.coe_lTensor (A := B)]
146	      exact Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap I.subtype
147	        Subtype.val_injective)
148	
149	set_option maxHeartbeats 1200000 in
150	-- Heartbeat headroom: several `IsLocalizedModule`/scalar-tower instance chains
151	-- at localized section modules (qcqs section-localization engine).
152	/-- **Chart-basic core of generic flatness.**  Let `Wj ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀` be an affine
153	chart whose section module localized at `g ∈ Γ(S, U₀)` is free over
154	`Γ(S, U₀)_g` (the output of algebraic generic freeness), and let `c ∈ Γ(X, Wj)`
155	cut a basic open lying above the base basic open `D(g)`.  Then the sections of
156	`F` over `D(c)` (presented as any open `O = D(c)`) are flat over `Γ(S, U₀)`.
157	
158	Route: `D(β) = Wj ⊓ p ⁻¹ᵁ D(g)` for `β := appLE g`; the section restriction
159	`Γ(F, Wj) → Γ(F, D(β))` is a localization at `powers β` over `Γ(X, Wj)`
160	(Stacks 01P0) hence at `powers g` over `Γ(S, U₀)` (base-ring descent), so
161	`Γ(F, D(β))` inherits freeness-hence-flatness over `Γ(S, U₀)` from the
162	hypothesis; finally `Γ(F, D(β)) → Γ(F, D(c))` is a fibre-side localization,
163	absorbed by mixed-base stability. -/
164	private theorem flat_section_chartBasic {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S)
165	    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent] {U₀ : S.Opens}
166	    {Wj : X.Opens} (hWj : IsAffineOpen Wj) (eWj : Wj ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀) (g : Γ(S, U₀))
167	    (hfree :
168	      letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, Wj) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom
169	      Module.Free (Localization.Away g)
170	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) Γ(F, Wj)))
171	    (c : Γ(X, Wj)) (hc : X.basicOpen c ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ (S.basicOpen g))
172	    {O : X.Opens} (hO : O = X.basicOpen c) (eO : O ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀) :
173	    letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ O eO).hom
174	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O) := by
175	  subst hO
176	  letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, Wj) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom
177	  -- the base element pushed into the chart ring, and its basic open
178	  set β : Γ(X, Wj) := (p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom g with hβ
179	  have hDβ : X.basicOpen β = Wj ⊓ p ⁻¹ᵁ (S.basicOpen g) := by
180	    rw [hβ, show (p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom g
181	        = (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE eWj).op).hom ((p.app U₀).hom g) from rfl]
182	    rw [Scheme.basicOpen_res, Scheme.preimage_basicOpen]
183	  have hDcβ : X.basicOpen c ≤ X.basicOpen β := by
184	    rw [hDβ]
185	    exact le_inf (X.basicOpen_le c) hc
186	  have eβ : X.basicOpen β ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ := (X.basicOpen_le β).trans eWj
187	  -- (1) chart-side localization of sections at β (Stacks 01P0)
188	  letI : Module Γ(X, Wj) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
189	    Module.compHom _ (algebraMap Γ(X, Wj) Γ(X, X.basicOpen β))
190	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(X, Wj) Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
191	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)
192	  haveI hlocB : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers β)
193	      (Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F β) :=
194	    Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isCompact F hWj.isCompact
195	      hWj.isQuasiSeparated β
196	  -- (2) `Γ(S, U₀)`-module structures and scalar towers
197	  letI : Algebra Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, Wj) := (p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom.toAlgebra
198	  letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
199	    Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ (X.basicOpen β) eβ).hom
200	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, Wj) Γ(F, Wj) :=
201	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)
202	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, Wj) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
203	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun a n => by
204	      change (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le β)).op).hom
205	          ((p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom a) • n
206	        = (p.appLE U₀ (X.basicOpen β) eβ).hom a • n
207	      rw [appLE_res_apply p eWj (X.basicOpen_le β) a])
208	  -- (3) base-ring descent of the localization, and flatness of `Γ(F, D(β))`
209	  haveI hlocA : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g)
210	      ((Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F β).restrictScalars Γ(S, U₀)) := by
211	    refine isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap g _ ?_
212	    exact hlocB
213	  haveI hfreeg : Module.Free (Localization.Away g)
214	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) Γ(F, Wj)) := hfree
215	  haveI hflatLoc : Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀)
216	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) Γ(F, Wj)) := by
217	    haveI : Module.Flat (Localization.Away g)
218	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) Γ(F, Wj)) := inferInstance
219	    exact (Module.flat_iff_of_isLocalization (Localization.Away g)
220	      (Submonoid.powers g) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) Γ(F, Wj))).mp this
221	  haveI hflatNβ : Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
222	    Module.Flat.of_linearEquiv
223	      (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers g)
224	        ((Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F β).restrictScalars Γ(S, U₀))).symm
225	  -- (4) restrict from `D(β)` to `D(c)` and absorb by mixed-base stability
226	  set c' : Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) :=
227	    (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le β)).op).hom c with hc'
228	  have hDc' : X.basicOpen c' = X.basicOpen c := by
229	    rw [hc', Scheme.basicOpen_res]
230	    exact inf_eq_right.mpr hDcβ
231	  have ec' : X.basicOpen c' ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ := (X.basicOpen_le c').trans eβ
232	  letI : Module Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) Γ(F, X.basicOpen c') :=
233	    Module.compHom _ (algebraMap Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) Γ(X, X.basicOpen c'))
234	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) Γ(X, X.basicOpen c')
235	      Γ(F, X.basicOpen c') :=

259	-- Heartbeat headroom: per-piece instance provisioning under binders for the

261	/-- **Two-layer basic-open reduction for generic flatness.**  Given affine charts
262	`Wc j ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀` covering `p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀`, all of whose section modules localized
263	at `f ∈ Γ(S, U₀)` are free (the output of algebraic generic freeness at a
264	common denominator `f`), every affine pair `U ≤ D(f)`, `W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U` has flat
265	sections: cover `W` by opens simultaneously basic in `W` and in a chart, lying
266	above base basic opens common to `U` and `U₀`; conclude with
267	`Module.flat_of_isLocalized_span` over `Γ(X, W)` and the two
268	`Module.flat_iff_of_isLocalization` exchanges on the base. -/
269	private theorem flat_section_pair {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules)
270	    [F.IsQuasicoherent] {U₀ : S.Opens} (hU₀ : IsAffineOpen U₀)
271	    {ι : Type u} (Wc : ι → X.Opens) (hWc : ∀ j, IsAffineOpen (Wc j))
272	    (eWc : ∀ j, Wc j ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀)
273	    (hcover : ∀ x ∈ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀, ∃ j, x ∈ Wc j) (f : Γ(S, U₀))
274	    (hfree : ∀ j,
275	      letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, Wc j) :=
276	        Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ (Wc j) (eWc j)).hom
277	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
278	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) Γ(F, Wc j)))
279	    {U : S.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (hUf : U ≤ S.basicOpen f)
280	    {W : X.Opens} (hW : IsAffineOpen W) (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
281	    letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U W e).hom
282	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := by
283	  letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U W e).hom
284	  have hUU₀ : U ≤ U₀ := hUf.trans (S.basicOpen_le f)
285	  have hWU₀ : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ := e.trans (fun x hx => hUU₀ hx)
286	  -- per-point choice of a simultaneous basic open
287	  have Hx : ∀ x : ↥W, ∃ (b : Γ(X, W)) (a : Γ(S, U)) (a' : Γ(S, U₀)) (j : ι)
288	      (c : Γ(X, Wc j)),
289	      X.basicOpen b = X.basicOpen c ∧ (x : X) ∈ X.basicOpen b ∧
290	      S.basicOpen a = S.basicOpen a' ∧
291	      X.basicOpen b ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ (S.basicOpen a) := by
292	    intro x
293	    have hxU : p.base x.1 ∈ U := e x.2
294	    have hxU₀ : p.base x.1 ∈ U₀ := hUU₀ hxU
295	    obtain ⟨a, a', haa', hpa⟩ :=
296	      AlgebraicGeometry.exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter hU hU₀ (p.base x.1)
297	        ⟨hxU, hxU₀⟩
298	    obtain ⟨j, hxj⟩ := hcover x.1 (hWU₀ x.2)
299	    have hxT : x.1 ∈ (W ⊓ (p ⁻¹ᵁ S.basicOpen a ⊓ Wc j) : X.Opens) :=
300	      ⟨x.2, ⟨hpa, hxj⟩⟩
301	    obtain ⟨b₁, hb₁le, hxb₁⟩ := hW.exists_basicOpen_le
302	      (⟨x.1, hxT⟩ : ↥(W ⊓ (p ⁻¹ᵁ S.basicOpen a ⊓ Wc j) : X.Opens)) x.2
303	    obtain ⟨f₁, c, hfc, hxf₁⟩ := AlgebraicGeometry.exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter
304	      (hW.basicOpen b₁) (hWc j) x.1 ⟨hxb₁, hxj⟩
305	    obtain ⟨b, hb⟩ := hW.basicOpen_basicOpen_is_basicOpen b₁ f₁
306	    exact ⟨b, a, a', j, c, hb.trans hfc, hb.symm ▸ hxf₁, haa',
307	      hb.symm ▸ ((X.basicOpen_le f₁).trans
308	        (hb₁le.trans (inf_le_right.trans inf_le_left)))⟩
309	  choose bb aa aa' jj cc hbc hxb haa' hba using Hx
310	  -- the covering family spans `Γ(X, W)`
311	  have hspan : Ideal.span (Set.range bb) = ⊤ := by
312	    rw [← hW.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff]
313	    apply le_antisymm
314	    · exact iSup_le fun r => X.basicOpen_le _
315	    · intro y hy
316	      exact TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mpr
317	        ⟨⟨bb ⟨y, hy⟩, Set.mem_range_self _⟩, hxb ⟨y, hy⟩⟩
318	  -- instance packs for the span lemma
319	  letI : Algebra Γ(S, U) Γ(X, W) := (p.appLE U W e).hom.toAlgebra
320	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(S, U) Γ(X, W) Γ(F, W) :=
321	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
322	  letI instBpiece : ∀ r : Set.range bb, Module Γ(X, W) Γ(F, X.basicOpen r.1) :=
323	    fun r => Module.compHom _ (algebraMap Γ(X, W) Γ(X, X.basicOpen r.1))
324	  haveI instTowerB : ∀ r : Set.range bb,
325	      IsScalarTower Γ(X, W) Γ(X, X.basicOpen r.1) Γ(F, X.basicOpen r.1) :=
326	    fun r => IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
327	  haveI instLoc : ∀ r : Set.range bb, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers r.1)
328	      (Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F r.1) := fun r =>
329	    Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isCompact F hW.isCompact
330	      hW.isQuasiSeparated r.1
331	  letI instRpiece : ∀ r : Set.range bb, Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, X.basicOpen r.1) :=
332	    fun r =>
333	      Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U (X.basicOpen r.1) ((X.basicOpen_le r.1).trans e)).hom
334	  haveI instTowerR : ∀ r : Set.range bb,
335	      IsScalarTower Γ(S, U) Γ(X, W) Γ(F, X.basicOpen r.1) := fun r =>
336	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun u n => by
337	      change (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le r.1)).op).hom
338	          ((p.appLE U W e).hom u) • n
339	        = (p.appLE U (X.basicOpen r.1) ((X.basicOpen_le r.1).trans e)).hom u • n
340	      rw [appLE_res_apply p e (X.basicOpen_le r.1) u]
341	  -- assemble by the span criterion; the pieces are handled below
342	  refine Module.flat_of_isLocalized_span (R := Γ(S, U)) Γ(X, W) Γ(F, W)
343	    (Set.range bb) hspan (fun r => Γ(F, X.basicOpen r.1))
344	    (fun r => Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F r.1) ?_
345	  -- per-piece flatness

348	  have hbar : X.basicOpen r.1 ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ (S.basicOpen (aa x)) := hx ▸ hba x
349	  have hbcr : X.basicOpen r.1 = X.basicOpen (cc x) := hx ▸ hbc x
350	  have epieceU₀ : X.basicOpen r.1 ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ := (X.basicOpen_le r.1).trans hWU₀
351	  have haU : S.basicOpen (aa x) ≤ U := S.basicOpen_le (aa x)
352	  have haU₀ : S.basicOpen (aa x) ≤ U₀ :=
353	    (haa' x).symm ▸ S.basicOpen_le (aa' x)
354	  -- module structures over the three base rings

358	    Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ (X.basicOpen r.1) epieceU₀).hom

322	/-- Given an `R`-algebra `S` and an `R`-module `M`, an `S`-module `N` together with a map
323	`f : M →ₗ[R] N` is the base change of `M` to `S` if the map `S × M → N, (s, m) ↦ s • f m` is the
324	tensor product. -/
325	def IsBaseChange : Prop :=
326	  IsTensorProduct
327	    (((Algebra.linearMap S <| Module.End S (M →ₗ[R] N)).flip f).restrictScalars R)

337	`M` to an `S`-module factors through `f`. -/
338	noncomputable nonrec def IsBaseChange.lift (g : M →ₗ[R] Q) : N →ₗ[S] Q :=
339	  { h.lift
340	      (((Algebra.linearMap S <| Module.End S (M →ₗ[R] Q)).flip g).restrictScalars R) with
341	    map_smul' := fun r x => by
342	      let F := ((Algebra.linearMap S <| Module.End S (M →ₗ[R] Q)).flip g).restrictScalars R
343	      have hF : ∀ (s : S) (m : M), h.lift F (s • f m) = s • g m := h.lift_eq F
344	      change h.lift F (r • x) = r • h.lift F x
345	      induction x using h.inductionOn with
346	      | zero => rw [smul_zero, map_zero, smul_zero]
347	      | tmul s m =>
348	        change h.lift F (r • s • f m) = r • h.lift F (s • f m)
349	        rw [← mul_smul, hF, hF, mul_smul]
350	      | add x₁ x₂ e₁ e₂ => rw [map_add, smul_add, map_add, smul_add, e₁, e₂] }

409	/-! ## §2. Generic flatness (Nitsure §4)
410	
411	Over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type
412	`X ⟶ S` is flat above some non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. This is the inductive
413	engine of the flattening-stratification theorem: combined with
414	Noetherian induction on the closed complement `S ∖ V`, it produces the
415	finite stratification of `S` by flatness loci.
416	
417	Algebraically (theorem `generic_flatness_algebraic`, no Lean pin): for a
418	noetherian domain `A`, a finite-type `A`-algebra `B`, and a finite
419	`B`-module `M`, there exists a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that `M_f` is a
420	free `A_f`-module. The geometric form (this declaration) restricts to a
421	non-empty affine open `Spec A ⊆ S` and applies the algebraic form on
422	each finite-type-algebra patch of `X` above `Spec A`.
423	
424	Blueprint reference: `thm:generic_flatness` (Nitsure §4). -/
425	
426	/-- **Generic flatness theorem, quasi-coherent core** (Nitsure §4).
427	
428	For a noetherian integral scheme `S`, a finite-type morphism `p : X ⟶ S`,
429	and a quasi-coherent `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕` equipped with a supply of affine
430	charts with finite section modules (`hfin` — for finitely presented `𝓕`
431	this is Stacks 01PC, see `genericFlatness` below; for pulled-back sheaves
432	in the flattening-stratification induction it comes from
433	`finite_section_pullback_piece`), there exists a non-empty open subscheme
434	`V ⊆ S` such that `𝓕|_{X_V} = 𝓕|_{p⁻¹V}` is flat over `𝓞_V`.
435	
436	PROVED (run 0010, T12 r4), following Nitsure §4: pass to a non-empty affine
437	open `U₀ ⊆ S` with `A := Γ(S, U₀)` a noetherian domain, cover the compact
438	preimage `p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀` by finitely many affine charts with finitely generated
439	sections (`Scheme.Modules.exists_affine_finite_sections_nhds`, Stacks 01PC),
440	apply algebraic generic freeness (`GenericFreeness.genericFlatnessAlgebraic`)
441	on each chart, and take `f ∈ A` the product of the witnesses.  The witness
442	open is `V := D(f)`, non-empty since `A` is a domain and `f ≠ 0`; the
443	quantified flatness on affine pairs below `V` is `flat_section_pair` (§1b).
444	
445	Statement repair (run 0010, T12 r2): Nitsure requires `p` of **finite type**
446	(EGA sense: quasi-compact AND locally of finite type). With
447	`LocallyOfFiniteType` alone the statement is FALSE: for
448	`X = ⨿_q Spec 𝔽_q → Spec ℤ` (locally of finite type, not quasi-compact,
449	structure sheaf finitely presented) every non-empty open `V ⊆ Spec ℤ`
450	contains all but finitely many primes `q`, and `𝔽_q` is never flat over the
451	corresponding `ℤ[1/n]`. Quasi-compactness is what bounds the number of
452	denominators to clear. Hence the `[QuasiCompact p]` hypothesis below. -/
453	theorem genericFlatness_of_finite_sections {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral S]
454	    [IsLocallyNoetherian S] (p : X ⟶ S) [QuasiCompact p] [LocallyOfFiniteType p]
455	    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
456	    (hfin : ∀ (x : X) (O : X.Opens), x ∈ O → ∃ V : X.Opens, IsAffineOpen V ∧
457	      x ∈ V ∧ V ≤ O ∧ Module.Finite Γ(X, V) Γ(F, V)) :
458	    ∃ (V : S.Opens), (V : Set S).Nonempty ∧
459	      ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (_ : U ≤ V) {W : X.Opens}
460	        (_ : IsAffineOpen W) (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U),
461	        letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U W e).hom
462	        Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := by
463	  classical
464	  -- an ambient nonempty affine chart of the integral base
465	  obtain ⟨x₀⟩ : Nonempty S := inferInstance
466	  obtain ⟨_, ⟨U₀, hU₀, rfl⟩, hx₀U₀, -⟩ :=
467	    S.isBasis_affineOpens.exists_subset_of_mem_open (Set.mem_univ x₀) isOpen_univ
468	  haveI : IsDomain Γ(S, U₀) := @IsIntegral.component_integral _ _ _ ⟨⟨x₀, hx₀U₀⟩⟩
469	  haveI : IsNoetherianRing Γ(S, U₀) :=
470	    IsLocallyNoetherian.component_noetherian ⟨U₀, hU₀⟩
471	  -- affine charts with finitely generated sections covering the compact preimage
472	  have hK : IsCompact ((p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ : X.Opens) : Set X) :=
473	    p.isCompact_preimage hU₀.isCompact
474	  have HX : ∀ x : X, x ∈ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ → ∃ V : X.Opens, IsAffineOpen V ∧ x ∈ V ∧
475	      V ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ ∧ Module.Finite Γ(X, V) Γ(F, V) := fun x hx =>
476	    hfin x (p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀) hx
477	  choose! V hVaff hxV hVle hVfin using HX
478	  obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := hK.elim_finite_subcover
479	    (fun x : ↥(p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ : X.Opens) => ((V x.1 : X.Opens) : Set X))
480	    (fun x => (V x.1).2)
481	    (fun y hy => Set.mem_iUnion.mpr ⟨⟨y, hy⟩, hxV y hy⟩)
482	  -- per-chart generic freeness over `A := Γ(S, U₀)`
483	  have hGF : ∀ i : ↥t, ∃ fi : Γ(S, U₀), fi ≠ 0 ∧
484	      (letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, V i.1.1) :=
485	        Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ (V i.1.1) (hVle i.1.1 i.1.2)).hom
486	      Module.Free (Localization.Away fi)
487	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers fi) Γ(F, V i.1.1))) := by
488	    intro i
489	    letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, V i.1.1) :=
490	      Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ (V i.1.1) (hVle i.1.1 i.1.2)).hom
491	    letI : Algebra Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, V i.1.1) :=
492	      (p.appLE U₀ (V i.1.1) (hVle i.1.1 i.1.2)).hom.toAlgebra
493	    haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, V i.1.1) Γ(F, V i.1.1) :=
494	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
495	    haveI hft : Algebra.FiniteType Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, V i.1.1) :=
496	      HasRingHomProperty.appLE (P := @LocallyOfFiniteType) p ‹_› ⟨U₀, hU₀⟩
497	        ⟨V i.1.1, hVaff i.1.1 i.1.2⟩ (hVle i.1.1 i.1.2)
498	    haveI := hVfin i.1.1 i.1.2
499	    exact GenericFreeness.genericFlatnessAlgebraic Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, V i.1.1)
500	      Γ(F, V i.1.1)
501	  choose ff hff0 hffree using hGF
502	  -- the common denominator
503	  set f : Γ(S, U₀) := ∏ i ∈ (Finset.univ : Finset ↥t), ff i with hfdef
504	  have hf0 : f ≠ 0 := by
505	    rw [hfdef]
506	    exact Finset.prod_ne_zero_iff.mpr fun i _ => hff0 i
507	  refine ⟨S.basicOpen f, ?_, ?_⟩
508	  · -- non-emptiness: on a reduced scheme `D(f) = ⊥` forces `f = 0`
509	    rw [← TopologicalSpace.Opens.ne_bot_iff_nonempty]
510	    intro hbot
511	    exact hf0 ((basicOpen_eq_bot_iff f).mp hbot)
512	  · -- flatness on every affine pair below `D(f)`, via the two-layer reduction
513	    intro U hU hUf W hW e
514	    refine flat_section_pair p F hU₀ (fun i : ↥t => V i.1.1)
515	      (fun i => hVaff i.1.1 i.1.2) (fun i => hVle i.1.1 i.1.2) ?_ f ?_ hU hUf hW e
516	    · -- the charts cover `p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀`
517	      intro x hx
518	      have := ht hx
519	      rw [Set.mem_iUnion₂] at this
520	      obtain ⟨i, hi, hxi⟩ := this
521	      exact ⟨⟨i, hi⟩, hxi⟩
522	    · -- freeness at the common denominator, chart by chart
523	      intro i
524	      letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, V i.1.1) :=
525	        Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ (V i.1.1) (hVle i.1.1 i.1.2)).hom
526	      have h1 := GenericFreeness.free_localizedModule_powers_mul Γ(S, U₀)
527	        Γ(F, V i.1.1) (ff i) (∏ j ∈ Finset.univ.erase i, ff j) (hffree i)
528	      rwa [Finset.mul_prod_erase _ _ (Finset.mem_univ i)] at h1
529	
530	/-- **Generic flatness theorem**, finitely-presented form (Nitsure §4).  The
531	statement of `thm:generic_flatness`: the chart supply of
532	`genericFlatness_of_finite_sections` is discharged by Stacks 01PC
533	(`Scheme.Modules.exists_affine_finite_sections_nhds`). -/
534	theorem genericFlatness {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral S] [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
535	    (p : X ⟶ S) [QuasiCompact p] [LocallyOfFiniteType p] (F : X.Modules)
536	    [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
537	    ∃ (V : S.Opens), (V : Set S).Nonempty ∧
538	      ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (_ : U ≤ V) {W : X.Opens}
539	        (_ : IsAffineOpen W) (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U),
540	        letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U W e).hom
541	        Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) :=
542	  genericFlatness_of_finite_sections p F
543	    (fun x O hxO => Scheme.Modules.exists_affine_finite_sections_nhds F x O hxO)
544	
545	/-! ## §3. Flatness under pushout base change (algebra core)
546	
547	Let `R → S` and `R → A` be ring maps with pushout `B = S ⊗[R] A`
548	(`Algebra.IsPushout R S A B`), and let `M` be an `A`-module that is flat
549	over `R`.  Then `B ⊗[A] M` — and hence any base change of `M` along
550	`A → B` — is flat over `S`.  This is the module form of "flatness is
551	stable under arbitrary base change" (Stacks 00HI item (3), relative
552	form).  Mathlib has the special case `Module.Flat.baseChange`
553	(`A = R`, `B = S`) and the ring form
554	`RingHom.Flat.isStableUnderBaseChange`, but not this mixed module form.
555	
556	The engine is `isBaseChange_pushout_tensorProduct`: `B ⊗[A] M` *is* the
557	base change of `M` along `R → S`, i.e. `B ⊗[A] M = S ⊗[R] M`.  The proof
558	is by the universal property (`IsBaseChange.of_lift_unique`): an

596	include hU in

599	  rw [← hU.fromSpec_image_basicOpen, Scheme.Hom.isAffineOpen_iff_of_isOpenImmersion]
600	  convert!
601	    isAffineOpen_opensRange
602	      (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom <| algebraMap Γ(X, U) (Localization.Away f)))
603	  exact Opens.ext (PrimeSpectrum.localization_away_comap_range (Localization.Away f) f).symm

606	    IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R) (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f) :=
607	  basicOpen_eq_of_affine f ▸ (isAffineOpen_top (Spec <| .of R)).basicOpen _

615	  apply (X.basicOpen r).ι.isAffineOpen_iff_of_isOpenImmersion.mp
616	  rw [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι, inf_comm,
617	    ← Scheme.basicOpen_res _ _ (homOfLE le_top).op]
618	  exact hU.basicOpen _
619	
620	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
621	include hU in
622	theorem exists_basicOpen_le {V : X.Opens} (x : V) (h : ↑x ∈ U) :
623	    ∃ f : Γ(X, U), X.basicOpen f ≤ V ∧ ↑x ∈ X.basicOpen f := by
624	  have : IsAffine _ := hU
625	  obtain ⟨_, ⟨_, ⟨r, rfl⟩, rfl⟩, h₁, h₂ : _ ≤ U.ι ⁻¹ᵁ V⟩ :=
626	    (isBasis_basicOpen U).exists_subset_of_mem_open (x.2 : (⟨x, h⟩ : U) ∈ _) (U.ι ⁻¹ᵁ V).isOpen
627	  replace h₁ : x.1 ∈ X.basicOpen r := by simpa [U.mem_basicOpen_toScheme] using! h₁
628	  replace h₂ : X.basicOpen r ≤ V := by
629	    simpa [Scheme.image_basicOpen] using! (U.ι.image_mono h₂).trans (U.ι.image_preimage_le _)
630	  exact ⟨U.topIso.hom.hom r, by simp [Scheme.Opens.toScheme_presheaf_obj, h₁, h₂]⟩
631	
632	noncomputable
633	instance {R : CommRingCat} {U} : Algebra R Γ(Spec R, U) :=
634	  inferInstanceAs (Algebra R ((Spec.structureSheaf R).presheaf.obj _))
635	
636	@[simp]
637	lemma algebraMap_Spec_obj {R : CommRingCat} {U} : algebraMap R Γ(Spec R, U) =
638	    ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso R).inv ≫ (Spec R).presheaf.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom := rfl
639	
640	instance {R : CommRingCat} {f : R} :
641	    IsLocalization.Away f Γ(Spec R, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f) :=
642	  inferInstanceAs (IsLocalization.Away f
643	    ((Spec.structureSheaf R).obj.obj (op <| PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))

645	/-- Given an affine open U and some `f : U`,
646	this is the canonical map `Γ(𝒪ₓ, D(f)) ⟶ Γ(Spec 𝒪ₓ(U), D(f))`
647	This is an isomorphism, as witnessed by an `IsIso` instance. -/
648	def basicOpenSectionsToAffine :
649	    Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) ⟶ Γ(Spec Γ(X, U), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f) :=
650	  hU.fromSpec.app (X.basicOpen f) ≫
651	    (Spec Γ(X, U)).presheaf.map (eqToHom (hU.fromSpec_preimage_basicOpen f).symm).op
652	
653	instance basicOpenSectionsToAffine_isIso :
654	    IsIso (basicOpenSectionsToAffine hU f) :=
655	  (hU.fromSpec.isIso_app _ (hU.opensRange_fromSpec.symm ▸ X.basicOpen_le f)).comp_isIso'
656	    inferInstance
657	
658	include hU in
659	theorem isLocalization_basicOpen :
660	    IsLocalization.Away f Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) := by
661	  apply
662	    (IsLocalization.isLocalization_iff_of_ringEquiv (Submonoid.powers f)
663	      (asIso <| basicOpenSectionsToAffine hU f).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv).mpr
664	  convert! StructureSheaf.IsLocalization.to_basicOpen _ f using 1
665	  apply Algebra.algebra_ext
666	  intro _
667	  congr 1
668	  dsimp [CommRingCat.ofHom, RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra, ← CommRingCat.hom_comp,
669	    basicOpenSectionsToAffine]
670	  rw [hU.fromSpec.naturality_assoc, hU.fromSpec_app_self]
671	  rfl
672	
673	instance _root_.AlgebraicGeometry.isLocalization_away_of_isAffine
674	    [IsAffine X] (r : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
675	    IsLocalization.Away r Γ(X, X.basicOpen r) :=
676	  isLocalization_basicOpen (isAffineOpen_top X) r
677	
678	lemma appLE_eq_away_map {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) {U : Y.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
679	    {V : X.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (e) (r : Γ(Y, U)) :
680	    letI := hU.isLocalization_basicOpen r
681	    letI := hV.isLocalization_basicOpen (f.appLE U V e r)
682	    f.appLE (Y.basicOpen r) (X.basicOpen (f.appLE U V e r)) (by simp [Scheme.Hom.appLE]) =
683	        CommRingCat.ofHom (IsLocalization.Away.map _ _ (f.appLE U V e).hom r) := by
684	  letI := hU.isLocalization_basicOpen r
685	  letI := hV.isLocalization_basicOpen (f.appLE U V e r)
686	  ext : 1
687	  apply IsLocalization.ringHom_ext (.powers r)
688	  rw [IsLocalization.Away.map, CommRingCat.hom_ofHom, IsLocalization.map_comp,
689	    RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra, RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra, ← CommRingCat.hom_comp,
690	    ← CommRingCat.hom_comp, Scheme.Hom.appLE_map, Scheme.Hom.map_appLE]
691	
692	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
693	lemma app_basicOpen_eq_away_map {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) {U : Y.Opens}
694	    (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (h : IsAffineOpen (f ⁻¹ᵁ U)) (r : Γ(Y, U)) :
695	    haveI := hU.isLocalization_basicOpen r
696	    haveI := h.isLocalization_basicOpen (f.app U r)
697	    f.app (Y.basicOpen r) =
698	      (CommRingCat.ofHom
699	        (IsLocalization.Away.map Γ(Y, Y.basicOpen r) Γ(X, X.basicOpen (f.app U r)) (f.app U).hom r)
700	        ≫ X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op) := by
701	  haveI := hU.isLocalization_basicOpen r
702	  haveI := h.isLocalization_basicOpen (f.app U r)
703	  ext : 1
704	  apply IsLocalization.ringHom_ext (.powers r)
705	  rw [IsLocalization.Away.map, CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc, CommRingCat.hom_ofHom,
706	    IsLocalization.map_comp, RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra,
707	    RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra, ← RingHom.comp_assoc, ← CommRingCat.hom_comp,
708	    ← CommRingCat.hom_comp, ← X.presheaf.map_comp]
709	  simp
710	
711	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
712	/-- `f.app (Y.basicOpen r)` is isomorphic to map induced on localizations
713	`Γ(Y, Y.basicOpen r) ⟶ Γ(X, X.basicOpen (f.app U r))` -/
714	def appBasicOpenIsoAwayMap {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) {U : Y.Opens}
715	    (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (h : IsAffineOpen (f ⁻¹ᵁ U)) (r : Γ(Y, U)) :
716	    haveI := hU.isLocalization_basicOpen r
717	    haveI := h.isLocalization_basicOpen (f.app U r)
718	    Arrow.mk (f.app (Y.basicOpen r)) ≅
719	      Arrow.mk (CommRingCat.ofHom (IsLocalization.Away.map Γ(Y, Y.basicOpen r)
720	        Γ(X, X.basicOpen (f.app U r)) (f.app U).hom r)) :=
721	  Arrow.isoMk (Iso.refl _) (X.presheaf.mapIso (eqToIso (by simp)).op) <| by
722	    simp [hU.app_basicOpen_eq_away_map f h]
723	    rfl
724	
725	include hU in
726	theorem isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen {V : X.Opens} (i : V ⟶ U) (e : V = X.basicOpen f) :
727	    @IsLocalization.Away _ _ f Γ(X, V) _ (X.presheaf.map i.op).hom.toAlgebra := by
728	  subst e; exact isLocalization_basicOpen hU f
729	
730	instance _root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Γ_restrict_isLocalization
731	    (X : Scheme.{u}) [IsAffine X] (r : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
732	    IsLocalization.Away r Γ(X.basicOpen r, ⊤) :=
733	  (isAffineOpen_top X).isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen r _ (Opens.isOpenEmbedding_obj_top _)
734	
735	include hU in
736	theorem basicOpen_basicOpen_is_basicOpen (g : Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)) :
737	    ∃ f' : Γ(X, U), X.basicOpen f' = X.basicOpen g := by
738	  have := isLocalization_basicOpen hU f
739	  obtain ⟨x, ⟨_, n, rfl⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalization.surj'' (Submonoid.powers f) g
740	  use f * x
741	  rw [Algebra.smul_def, Scheme.basicOpen_mul, Scheme.basicOpen_mul, RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra,
742	    Scheme.basicOpen_res]
743	  refine (inf_eq_left.mpr (inf_le_left.trans_eq (Scheme.basicOpen_of_isUnit _ ?_).symm)).symm
744	  exact
745	    Submonoid.leftInv_le_isUnit _
746	      (IsLocalization.toInvSubmonoid (Submonoid.powers f) (Γ(X, X.basicOpen f))
747	        _).prop
748	
749	include hU in
750	theorem _root_.AlgebraicGeometry.exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter
751	    {V : X.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (x : X) (hx : x ∈ U ⊓ V) :
752	    ∃ (f : Γ(X, U)) (g : Γ(X, V)), X.basicOpen f = X.basicOpen g ∧ x ∈ X.basicOpen f := by
753	  obtain ⟨f, hf₁, hf₂⟩ := hU.exists_basicOpen_le ⟨x, hx.2⟩ hx.1
754	  obtain ⟨g, hg₁, hg₂⟩ := hV.exists_basicOpen_le ⟨x, hf₂⟩ hx.2
755	  obtain ⟨f', hf'⟩ :=
756	    basicOpen_basicOpen_is_basicOpen hU f (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hf₁ : _ ⟶ V).op g)
757	  replace hf' := (hf'.trans (RingedSpace.basicOpen_res _ _ _)).trans (inf_eq_right.mpr hg₁)
758	  exact ⟨f', g, hf', hf'.symm ▸ hg₂⟩
